description: Reference identifier or hash of the digital signature captured when consent_method is 'digital'. Links to the secure digital signature storage system. Not the signature itself — a reference token for audit trail purposes.
reference: GDPR Article 7; eIDAS Regulation (EU Electronic Signatures); Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4

description: The date from which the consent becomes legally and operationally binding. May differ from consent_date if there is a processing delay or a future-dated consent arrangement.
reference: GDPR Article 7; Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4

description: The date on which the consent expires and must be renewed. Nullable for open-ended consents. Supports automated consent renewal workflows and compliance with data retention policies.
reference: GDPR Article 5(1)(e) (Storage Limitation); Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4; IATI Standard

description: File path, URL, or document management system reference to the scanned or digital copy of the signed consent form. Enables retrieval of the original consent document for audit and legal purposes.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; GDPR Article 7

description: Indicates whether the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) applies to this consent record based on the beneficiary's location, the organization's jurisdiction, or the data controller's domicile. True = GDPR applies; False = GDPR does not apply.
reference: GDPR Article 3 (Territorial Scope); GDPR Article 6 (Lawfulness of Processing)

description: Indicates whether a supervisor or quality assurance officer has verified that the consent was truly informed (i.e., the beneficiary understood the purpose, scope, and their rights before consenting). True = verified; False = pending verification.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4; SPHERE Humanitarian Standards; HAP (Humanitarian Accountability Partnership) Standard

description: Indicates whether consent was given by a proxy (guardian, parent, or authorized representative) on behalf of a minor, elderly, or incapacitated beneficiary. True = proxy consent; False = direct consent from the beneficiary.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4; SPHERE Humanitarian Standards; GDPR Article 8 (Conditions for Child Consent)

description: Free-text field for additional contextual notes about the consent process, any special circumstances, beneficiary concerns raised, or follow-up actions required. Used by field staff and MEL officers for qualitative documentation.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4; HAP (Humanitarian Accountability Partnership) Standard

business_glossary_term: Photography and Media Use Permitted Flag
description: Indicates whether the beneficiary has consented to being photographed or filmed for organizational communications, donor reporting, or advocacy purposes. True = photography/media use permitted; False = not permitted.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4; HAP (Humanitarian Accountability Partnership) Standard; GDPR Article 9

description: Full name of the proxy individual (guardian, parent, or authorized representative) who provided consent on behalf of the beneficiary. Populated only when is_proxy_consent is True.
reference: GDPR Article 8; Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4; SPHERE Humanitarian Standards

description: The relationship of the proxy consent giver to the beneficiary (e.g., parent, guardian, legal representative). Populated only when is_proxy_consent is True. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: parent|guardian|spouse|sibling|community_leader|legal_representative|other — promote to reference product]
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4; GDPR Article 8

description: Indicates whether the beneficiary has consented to their data being shared with third-party organizations (e.g., partner NGOs, UN agencies, government bodies). True = sharing permitted; False = sharing not permitted. Critical for inter-agency data sharing compliance.
reference: GDPR Article 6; Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4; IATI Standard; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: Free-text description of any specific restrictions or conditions placed by the beneficiary on data sharing (e.g., 'not to be shared with government authorities', 'only for health program purposes'). Populated when sharing_permitted is True but with conditions.
reference: GDPR Article 7; Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4; HAP (Humanitarian Accountability Partnership) Standard

description: The unique identifier of this consent record in the originating operational system (CommCare case ID, KoboToolbox submission UUID, Dynamics 365 record ID). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and source systems.
reference: IATI Standard; Internal Data Lineage and Governance Policy

description: The operational system of record from which this consent record was captured or ingested (e.g., CommCare for mobile case management, KoboToolbox for field surveys, Dynamics 365 for case management, Salesforce for constituent tracking, or manual for paper-based entry).
reference: IATI Standard; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; Internal Data Governance Policy

description: The date and time when this consent record was most recently modified. Tracks consent status changes, withdrawals, and verification updates for audit trail and data governance purposes.
reference: GDPR Article 5(2) (Accountability Principle); Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: The date on which a supervisor or quality assurance officer verified that the consent was properly informed and documented. Populated only when informed_consent_verified is True.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: The date on which the beneficiary formally withdrew their previously given consent. Populated only when consent_status is 'withdrawn'. Triggers downstream data deletion or anonymization workflows per GDPR right to erasure.
reference: GDPR Article 7(3) (Right to Withdraw Consent); Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4

description: Free-text description of the reason provided by the beneficiary or their representative for withdrawing consent. Populated only when consent_status is 'withdrawn'. Used for accountability reporting and program improvement.
reference: GDPR Article 7(3); Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4; HAP (Humanitarian Accountability Partnership) Standard

description: Full name of the field staff member or community witness who witnessed the consent process. Required for verbal and thumbprint consent methods to ensure accountability and legal validity.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4; HAP (Humanitarian Accountability Partnership) Standard; SPHERE Humanitarian Standards

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each needs assessment record. Primary key for the needs_assessment data product within the beneficiary domain.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Alliance — Data Management Principles

description: Foreign key linking to partnership.partnership_agreement. Business justification: Needs assessments are funded and scoped under specific partnership agreements; donor reporting requires attributing assessment coverage and methodology to the governing agreement. Partner performance

Foreign key linking to workforce.position. Business justification: Needs assessments are conducted by staff in specific positions (Nutrition Officer, Protection Officer, WASH Engineer). Linking to position enables assessment quality analysis by position type and staf

Foreign key linking to supply.commodity. Business justification: Needs assessments identify specific commodity requirements (NFI kits, food baskets, WASH items, shelter materials) for beneficiaries. Essential for humanitarian needs-based procurement planning and Sp
Reference to the community or settlement unit that is the subject of this assessment when assessment_level is 'community'. Nullable when assessment_level is 'individual' or 'household'.
reference: OCHA Common Operational Datasets — Settlement/Community Registry

description: Foreign key linking to program.component. Business justification: Needs assessments in NGOs are commissioned by specific program components to inform component-level design and targeting decisions. Component-level aggregation of assessment findings is required for c

Foreign key linking to compliance.corrective_action_plan. Business justification: When internal reviews or audits identify needs assessment quality issues (sampling bias, incomplete data, targeting errors), corrective action plans document remediation measures. Essential for qualit

The unique identifier of the data collection instrument used to conduct this assessment. For KoboToolbox, this is the form UID (e.g., 'aXmK3p9QrT'). For CommCare, this is the module ID. Enables traceability back to the specific questionnaire version and supports data quality audits.
reference: KoboToolbox Form Management — Form UID; CommCare Application Builder — Module ID

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.donor_requirement. Business justification: Needs assessments are frequently mandated by specific donor requirements (baseline assessments before program start, specific assessment tools, minimum sample sizes). Linking to donor_requirement enab

Foreign key linking to field.emergency. Business justification: Needs assessments are triggered by and scoped to specific emergencies (e.g., flood rapid assessment). Linking to emergency enables flash appeal and HRP needs analysis by emergency. No existing emergen

Foreign key linking to volunteer.volunteer. Business justification: Volunteers conduct needs assessments in many NGO operations (household surveys, vulnerability assessments, rapid assessments). Standard practice for community-based data collection. Required for track

Foreign key linking to field.field_team. Business justification: Field teams conduct needs assessments; supervisors need team-level assessment quality assurance and workload tracking. enumerator_volunteer_id covers individual enumerators but not the deploying field

Foreign key linking to finance.grant_budget. Business justification: Needs assessments are funded by specific grants; donor financial reporting requires tracking assessment activity costs against the grant budget. NGO finance officers report assessment expenditure per
Reference to the household unit that is the subject of this assessment when assessment_level is 'household'. Nullable when assessment_level is 'individual' or 'community'.
reference: SPHERE Handbook — Household-Level Assessment Standards; KoboToolbox Household Survey Module

description: Reference to the humanitarian or development program under which this needs assessment was commissioned. Links the assessment to the program's Theory of Change (ToC) and LogFrame targeting criteria.
reference: Microsoft Dynamics 365 for Nonprofits — Program Management Module; IATI Standard v2.03

description: Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Needs assessments are conducted by field teams under specific org units. Linking enables organizational aggregation of assessment findings for program planning, budget allocation, and donor reporting

Foreign key linking to partnership.partner_org. Business justification: Partners conduct needs assessments in their operational areas under consortium or sub-award arrangements. Tracking the assessing partner is required for data quality oversight, capacity assessment val
Reference to the staff member or volunteer who conducted the field assessment. Used for quality assurance, inter-rater reliability checks, and enumerator performance monitoring.
reference: CHS Alliance — Accountability to Affected Populations; CommCare Field Worker Module

description: Foreign key linking to field.project_site. Business justification: Needs assessments are conducted at specific project sites. Field coordinators filter assessments by site for program targeting and HRP reporting. No existing project_site_id on this product; admin1_na

Reference to the individual beneficiary (IDP, PoC, GBV survivor, or other target population member) who is the subject of this assessment when assessment_level is 'individual'. Nullable when assessment_level is 'household' or 'community'.
reference: CommCare Case Management — Beneficiary Case ID; UNHCR PoC Registration Standards

description: Foreign key linking to mel.reporting_period. Business justification: Baseline/midline/endline needs assessment alignment: NGO MEL frameworks require needs assessments to be explicitly tied to reporting periods (baseline, midline, endline) for longitudinal tracking of v

Foreign key linking to beneficiary.vulnerability_profile. Business justification: Needs assessments are often conducted in direct reference to an existing vulnerability profile, or they generate/update a vulnerability profile. Linking beneficiary_needs_assessment to vulnerability_p

Name of the first-level administrative division (e.g., province, state, region) where the assessment was conducted. Sourced from OCHA Common Operational Datasets (CODs) administrative boundary reference. Used for geographic aggregation in SitReps and donor reports.
reference: OCHA Common Operational Datasets (CODs) — Administrative Boundaries; IATI Country/Region Codes

description: Name of the second-level administrative division (e.g., district, county, department) where the assessment was conducted. Sourced from OCHA CODs. Enables sub-national geographic disaggregation for targeting and cluster coordination reporting.
reference: OCHA Common Operational Datasets (CODs) — Administrative Boundaries

description: The calendar date on which the needs assessment was physically conducted in the field. This is the principal real-world event date, distinct from the record submission or validation timestamps. Used for temporal targeting analysis and periodic reassessment scheduling.
reference: SPHERE Handbook — Needs Assessment Timing; OCHA Assessment Registry Standards

description: The unit of analysis at which this needs assessment was conducted. 'individual' targets a single beneficiary (e.g., GBV survivor, SAM case); 'household' targets a family unit; 'community' targets a settlement or geographic cluster. Determines which of beneficiary_id, household_id, or community_id is populated.
reference: SPHERE Handbook Chapter 1 — Needs Assessment Methodology; IASC Needs Assessment Task Force Guidelines

description: Externally-known alphanumeric reference code assigned to this needs assessment, used in field reports, SitReps, and donor communications. Format: NA-{COUNTRY}-{YEAR}-{SEQUENCE}. Sourced from KoboToolbox or CommCare submission ID.
reference: IATI Standard v2.03 — Activity Identifier; KoboToolbox Form Submission Reference

description: Current workflow lifecycle state of the needs assessment record. 'draft' indicates field data entry in progress; 'submitted' indicates enumerator submission pending review; 'validated' indicates supervisor-approved and usable for targeting; 'rejected' indicates data quality failure requiring re-assessment.
reference: CHS Alliance — Data Quality Standards; KoboToolbox Submission Workflow

description: Version number or deployment date of the assessment tool/form used. Critical for longitudinal comparability — when a KoboToolbox form or CommCare module is updated, findings from different versions may not be directly comparable without version-aware analysis.
reference: KoboToolbox Form Versioning; IASC Needs Assessment Task Force — Comparability Standards

description: Classification of the assessment's purpose and methodology. 'initial_registration' is conducted at first beneficiary contact; 'periodic_reassessment' is a scheduled follow-up to update vulnerability status; 'post_crisis_rapid' is an emergency rapid assessment following a shock event; 'sector_specific_deep' is an in-depth assessment for a single sector (e.g., nutrition MUAC screening, WASH facility survey).
reference: IASC Needs Assessment Task Force — Assessment Types Taxonomy; OCHA Assessment Registry

description: Number of children under 5 years of age in the assessed household. Key vulnerability indicator for nutrition (SAM/GAM screening), WASH (diarrhoea risk), and child protection programming. Required for UNICEF and WFP programme targeting.
reference: UNICEF Child Vulnerability Standards; SPHERE Handbook — Nutrition (Under-5 Focus); WFP Targeting Criteria

description: Indicates whether informed consent was obtained from the beneficiary or household head prior to conducting the assessment. Mandatory per CHS Commitment 4 (Accountability to Affected Populations) and GDPR Article 6 for data processing lawfulness. Must be TRUE for the record to be validated.
reference: CHS Alliance — Commitment 4 Accountability; GDPR Article 6 Lawful Basis; HAP Standard

description: The form of informed consent obtained. 'verbal' is spoken consent recorded by enumerator; 'written' is signed consent form; 'proxy' is consent given by a guardian or community leader on behalf of a vulnerable individual (e.g., child, person with disability). Relevant for legal compliance and data protection audits.
reference: GDPR Article 7 — Conditions for Consent; CHS Alliance Data Protection Guidelines; ICRC Data Protection in Humanitarian Action

description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the assessment was conducted (e.g., 'SOM', 'SSD', 'HTI'). Used for IATI reporting, donor country-level aggregation, and cross-country comparative analysis.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; IATI Standard v2.03 — Country Code

description: The date and time when this needs assessment record was first created in the data platform (Silver layer ingestion timestamp). Distinct from assessment_date (field event date). Used for data pipeline monitoring and audit trail compliance.
reference: FASB ASC 958 — Record Keeping; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 — Documentation Requirements

description: The primary method used to gather assessment data. 'face_to_face' is direct in-person interview; 'remote_phone' is telephone-based; 'fgd' is a Focus Group Discussion (FGD); 'kii' is a Key Informant Interview (KII); 'observation' is direct field observation without interview; 'secondary_data' is derived from existing records. Affects data quality weighting in vulnerability scoring.
reference: IASC Needs Assessment Task Force — Data Collection Methodology; SPHERE Handbook

description: The displacement classification of the assessed individual or household at the time of assessment. 'idp' = Internally Displaced Person (IDP); 'refugee' = person with international protection status; 'returnee' = person returning to place of origin; 'host_community' = non-displaced community member hosting displaced persons. Critical for UNHCR/OCHA population movement tracking.
reference: UNHCR Refugee Definition (1951 Convention); OCHA IDP Tracking Matrix (DTM); Guiding Principles on Internal Displacement

description: Numeric score for the education sector assessment findings. Null if education was not included in sectors_assessed. Covers school access, enrollment barriers, learning environment safety, and teacher availability against SPHERE/INEE minimum standards.
reference: SPHERE Handbook — Education; INEE Minimum Standards for Education in Emergencies; IASC Education Cluster

description: Free-text qualitative observations recorded by the field enumerator during the assessment. Captures contextual information not covered by structured questions (e.g., security constraints, access limitations, observed conditions). Used by supervisors during validation review.
reference: KoboToolbox — Note Question Type; CommCare — Case Note Field; CHS Alliance Data Quality Standards

description: Indicates whether the assessed household is headed by a female. Female-headed households are a standard vulnerability marker in humanitarian targeting criteria and are required for gender disaggregation in donor reports (USAID, DFID/FCDO, UN Women standards).
reference: USAID Gender Equality and Female Empowerment Policy; UN Women Humanitarian Action; IASC Gender Marker

description: Indicates whether the assessment identified elevated Gender-Based Violence (GBV) risk for the assessed individual or household. When TRUE, triggers referral to GBV case management pathway and PSS services. Classified as restricted PHI due to extreme sensitivity of GBV disclosure data.
reference: IASC GBV Guidelines; UNHCR GBVIMS Data Protection Protocol; SPHERE Handbook — Protection Principles

description: Estimated GPS accuracy in meters at the time of coordinate capture, as reported by the mobile device. KoboToolbox and CommCare record this automatically. Values above 30 meters may indicate unreliable location data and trigger data quality flags.
reference: KoboToolbox GPS Question Type — Accuracy Field; Humanitarian OpenStreetMap Data Quality Standards

description: Geographic latitude coordinate (WGS84 decimal degrees) of the assessment location captured by the field enumerator's device via KoboToolbox or CommCare GPS capture. Used for GIS mapping, cluster analysis, and geographic targeting in humanitarian response planning.
reference: Humanitarian OpenStreetMap — WGS84 Coordinate Standard; OCHA GIS Standards

description: Numeric score for the livelihoods sector assessment findings. Null if livelihoods was not included in sectors_assessed. Covers income sources, asset ownership, market access, and economic vulnerability indicators.
reference: SPHERE Handbook — Livelihoods; IASC Livelihoods Cluster Assessment Framework; USAID BHA Livelihoods Assessment Standards

description: Geographic longitude coordinate (WGS84 decimal degrees) of the assessment location captured by the field enumerator's device. Used alongside latitude for GIS mapping, spatial clustering, and geographic targeting in humanitarian response planning.
reference: Humanitarian OpenStreetMap — WGS84 Coordinate Standard; OCHA GIS Standards

description: Mid-Upper Arm Circumference (MUAC) measurement in millimetres for children under 5 or pregnant/lactating women, used to screen for acute malnutrition. MUAC < 115mm indicates SAM; 115–125mm indicates MAM. Classified as PHI as it is a direct health measurement linked to an individual. Null if not applicable to assessment subject.
reference: WHO MUAC Screening Protocol; SPHERE Handbook — Nutrition; UNICEF MUAC Standards

description: Numeric score for the nutrition sector assessment findings. Null if nutrition was not included in sectors_assessed. Incorporates Global Acute Malnutrition (GAM) and Severe Acute Malnutrition (SAM) indicators where applicable. Feeds into DHIS2 nutrition indicator tracking.
reference: SPHERE Handbook — Nutrition Chapter; WHO/UNICEF GAM/SAM Thresholds; DHIS2 Nutrition Indicators

description: Composite numeric score representing the assessed subject's overall vulnerability level, calculated from sector-specific findings using the program's scoring methodology (e.g., 0–100 scale). Feeds into vulnerability_profile updates when the score materially changes vulnerability classification. This is a raw assessment output, not a derived KPI.
reference: IASC Needs Assessment Task Force — Vulnerability Scoring; UNHCR Vulnerability Assessment Framework

description: Number of household members with a physical, sensory, intellectual, or psychosocial disability. Required for disability-inclusive programming per IASC Guidelines on Inclusion of Persons with Disabilities in Humanitarian Action and CRPD compliance reporting.
reference: IASC Guidelines on Inclusion of Persons with Disabilities; UN CRPD; Washington Group Short Set on Disability

description: Numeric score for the protection sector assessment findings. Null if protection was not included in sectors_assessed. Covers GBV risk, child protection, housing land and property rights, and documentation status. Sensitive findings are handled per GBV information management protocols.
reference: IASC Protection Cluster — Assessment Standards; UNHCR GBV Information Management System (GBVIMS); SPHERE Handbook — Protection Principles

description: Indicates whether the assessment findings recommend referral of the beneficiary or household to one or more service providers or program interventions. When TRUE, a referral pathway is initiated in CommCare case management. Drives downstream case management workflow.
reference: CHS Alliance — Commitment 3 (Referral Pathways); CommCare Case Management Referral Module

description: Pipe-delimited list of sectors to which the beneficiary or household is being referred based on assessment findings (e.g., 'nutrition|PSS|WASH'). Populated only when referral_recommended is TRUE. Drives service pathway activation in CommCare and Microsoft Dynamics 365 case management.
reference: CHS Alliance — Commitment 3 (Referral Pathways); IASC Cluster Referral Protocols; CommCare Referral Module

description: Pipe-delimited list of humanitarian/development sectors covered in this assessment (e.g., 'WASH|nutrition|shelter|protection|livelihoods|education'). Sectors align with IASC cluster system. Determines which sector-specific scored findings fields are populated. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WASH|nutrition|shelter|protection|livelihoods|education|health|food_security|NFI|PSS — promote to reference product]
reference: IASC Cluster System — Sector Taxonomy; SPHERE Handbook — Sector Chapters; OCHA Cluster Coordination

description: Numeric score for the shelter sector assessment findings. Null if shelter was not included in sectors_assessed. Assessed against SPHERE minimum standards for covered living space, structural safety, and thermal comfort.
reference: SPHERE Handbook — Shelter and Settlement Chapter; IASC Shelter Cluster Assessment Standards

description: The native submission or case ID from the originating source system (KoboToolbox submission UUID or CommCare case ID). Enables exact record traceability back to the source system for data reconciliation, duplicate detection, and audit purposes.
reference: KoboToolbox API — Submission UUID; CommCare API — Case ID; Databricks Silver Layer Lineage

description: The operational system of record from which this assessment record originated. Enables data lineage tracking and source-specific quality rules in the Silver layer. 'KoboToolbox' for field survey submissions; 'CommCare' for mobile case management assessments; 'manual_entry' for paper-based data entry.
reference: IATI Standard — Data Provenance; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer — Data Lineage Standards

description: Free-text notes entered by the supervising data manager or MEL officer during the assessment review and validation process. Documents reasons for rejection, data quality concerns, or approval rationale. Populated when assessment_status transitions to 'validated' or 'rejected'.
reference: CHS Alliance — Data Quality and Accountability Standards; MEAL Framework — Data Verification Protocols

description: The date and time when this needs assessment record was most recently modified in the data platform. Tracks post-ingestion corrections, validation status changes, and supervisor note additions. Required for Silver layer change data capture (CDC) processing.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer — CDC Standards; CHS Alliance Data Management Principles

description: The date and time when the assessment record was validated or rejected by the supervising staff member. Distinct from assessment_date (field event) and created_timestamp (record creation). Used for SLA monitoring of data quality review turnaround.
reference: CHS Alliance — Data Quality Standards; MEAL Framework

description: Categorical classification of the assessed subject's vulnerability level derived from the overall_vulnerability_score and program-specific thresholds. Used for beneficiary targeting, prioritization, and service referral decisions. 'critical' triggers immediate case management escalation.
reference: UNHCR Vulnerability Assessment Framework; IASC Needs Assessment Task Force — Vulnerability Classification

business_glossary_term: Water Sanitation and Hygiene (WASH) Sector Score
description: Numeric score for the Water, Sanitation and Hygiene (WASH) sector assessment findings. Null if WASH was not included in sectors_assessed. Scored against SPHERE minimum standards for water access, sanitation facilities, and hygiene promotion.
reference: SPHERE Handbook — WASH Chapter; IASC WASH Cluster Assessment Standards

description: Unique identifier for the protection flag record. Primary key.
Reference to the case management record if this flag is part of an active case. Links to CommCare case management system.
Reference to the geographic location where the protection concern was identified. Links to field operations location master.

Foreign key linking to compliance.donor_requirement. Business justification: Protection incidents trigger mandatory donor reporting requirements (USAID ADS 303, ECHO protection reporting, UNHCR incident reporting). Linking protection_flag to the specific donor_requirement mand

Foreign key linking to field.emergency. Business justification: Protection flags raised during emergencies must be tracked against the emergency for GBV/protection cluster reporting to OCHA and donors. Emergency-level protection flag counts are a standard humanita

Foreign key linking to compliance.governance_policy. Business justification: Protection flags must be handled per specific governance policies (safeguarding policy, GBV response policy, whistleblower policy). Linking protection_flag to the governing governance_policy ensures p

Foreign key linking to beneficiary.household. Business justification: Protection concerns in humanitarian contexts frequently apply at the household level (e.g., GBV risk for a female-headed household, child protection concerns affecting all household members). Adding h

Foreign key linking to compliance.incident. Business justification: Protection concerns (GBV, child abuse, exploitation, SEA) trigger formal compliance incidents requiring investigation per safeguarding policies. Essential for linking beneficiary protection flags to o
Reference to the humanitarian or development program under which this protection flag is being managed and tracked.

Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Protection flags are managed by protection teams within org units. Linking enables protection incident reporting by org unit for cluster coordination, safeguarding compliance reporting, and donor acco
Reference to the protection officer or case worker currently responsible for managing this protection flag and coordinating response.

Foreign key linking to field.project_site. Business justification: Protection flags are identified at specific project sites; field protection officers need site-level protection dashboards for cluster reporting. protection_flag already links to country_office but no
Reference to the beneficiary or household associated with this protection flag. Links to the beneficiary master record.

Foreign key linking to volunteer.volunteer. Business justification: Protection monitoring: volunteers (community protection monitors) identify and report protection flags. Tracking the reporting volunteer is required for accountability, supervision, and protection inc
Data classification level for this protection flag record. Most protection flags are Restricted due to sensitive nature of protection concerns.
Date when informed consent was obtained from the beneficiary or guardian for protection case management.
Indicates whether informed consent was obtained from the beneficiary or guardian for recording and managing this protection flag.
Timestamp when this protection flag record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data governance.
reference: Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) for Nonprofits

description: Name of the operational system from which this protection flag record originated. Typically CommCare, KoboToolbox, or partner case management systems.
reference: Information and Communication Technology for Development (ICT4D) Standards

description: Date when the protection flag was escalated to higher authority or specialized protection services.
Explanation of why the protection flag required escalation, including severity factors or complexity of the case.
Indicates whether this protection flag requires escalation to senior management, specialized protection teams, or external authorities.
External identifier from the source system or partner organization. Used for cross-system reconciliation and data integration.
Standardized code identifying the protection flag type. Used for system integration and reporting aggregation.
Date when the protection concern was first identified or flagged. Represents the business event timestamp for this protection record.
Severity level of the protection concern indicating urgency of response and level of risk to the beneficiary.
Current lifecycle status of the protection flag indicating whether it requires active intervention, ongoing monitoring, or has been resolved.

Category of protection concern. GBV (Gender-Based Violence), SGBV (Sexual and Gender-Based Violence), UASC (Unaccompanied and Separated Children). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GBV Risk|Child Protection|SGBV Survivor|Unaccompanied Minor|UASC|Trafficking Risk|Forced Recruitment|Disability Protection|Elderly at Risk|Medical Vulnerability — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Name of the organization, program, or system that originally flagged this protection concern. May reference partner organizations or internal programs.
Indicates whether ongoing follow-up monitoring or case management is required for this protection flag.

Method or channel through which the protection concern was identified. FGD (Focus Group Discussion), KII (Key Informant Interview). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Field Assessment|Community Referral|Self-Reported|Partner Organization|Health Screening|Household Survey|Focus Group Discussion|Key Informant Interview — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Description of immediate protection needs or interventions required to address the flagged concern. Used for rapid response planning.
Indicates whether this protection flag is currently active and requires ongoing attention. Derived from flag_status but provided for simplified querying.
Date of the most recent follow-up visit or assessment conducted for this protection flag.
Indicates whether the protection concern requires legal intervention, law enforcement involvement, or judicial proceedings.
Timestamp when this protection flag record was last modified. Audit trail field for data governance and change tracking.
reference: Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) for Nonprofits

description: Scheduled date for the next follow-up visit or assessment to monitor the protection concern and beneficiary status.
Additional case notes, observations, or contextual information related to the protection flag. Contains sensitive case management details.
Indicates whether Psychosocial Support (PSS) services have been provided to the beneficiary as part of protection response.
reference: Inter-Agency Standing Committee (IASC) Mental Health and Psychosocial Support Guidelines

description: Date when the referral to specialized protection services was made.
Indicates whether the beneficiary has been referred to specialized protection services or partner organizations for support.
Name of the organization or service provider to which the beneficiary was referred for specialized protection support.
Reporting period during which this protection flag was active or recorded. Format: YYYY-QN for quarterly or YYYY-MM for monthly reporting.
Date when the protection concern was resolved or the flag was closed. Null if the flag is still active or under monitoring.
Narrative description of how the protection concern was resolved, interventions provided, and outcomes achieved.
Detailed narrative description of the specific protection risk or concern. Contains sensitive information about the nature of the threat or vulnerability.
Unique identifier for the referral transaction. Primary key for the referral data product.

Foreign key linking to beneficiary.case_action. Business justification: Referrals in humanitarian case management are typically the outcome of a specific case action (e.g., a home visit action that identifies the need for a referral to a health facility). Linking referral
Identifier of the case management record associated with this referral, if applicable.

Foreign key linking to program.component. Business justification: Referrals in NGO case management are made from and to specific program components (e.g., nutrition component refers to health component). Component-level referral tracking is required for inter-compon

Foreign key linking to compliance.donor_requirement. Business justification: Donor requirements mandate specific referral pathways and protocols (USAID ADS 303 GBV referral standards, UNHCR protection referral requirements). Linking referral to the governing donor_requirement
Identifier of the specific program within the referring organization that is making the referral.

Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Referrals are processed by specific org units. Linking enables referral pathway analysis by organizational unit, used in protection cluster coordination, inter-agency referral reporting, and donor nar

Foreign key linking to field.project_site. Business justification: Referrals specify destination field facilities for service delivery. Core to protection and health referral pathway management, inter-agency coordination, and service accessibility analysis. Role-pref
Identifier of the organization or service provider that is receiving and expected to act on the referral.
Identifier of the staff member at the receiving organization who is assigned to handle this referral.
Identifier of the organization or partner that is initiating and submitting the referral.
Identifier of the staff member or case worker who initiated the referral on behalf of the beneficiary.

business_glossary_term: International Aid Transparency Initiative (IATI) Activity ID

reference: IATI Standard v2.03; International Aid Transparency Initiative

description: Indicates whether this amendment constitutes a no-cost extension (NCE), extending the period of performance without additional funding. True = NCE; False = other amendment type. Critical for grant closeout planning and donor reporting.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.308; USAID ADS 303

description: Narrative explanation of the business rationale for the amendment, including the circumstances that necessitated the change (e.g., context changes, operational constraints, donor-directed modifications). Required for donor reporting and audit compliance under 2 CFR 200.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.308; CHS Alliance Core Humanitarian Standard

description: Full name of the authorized representative of Ngo who signs this amendment on behalf of the organization. Required for legal enforceability and signatory change tracking.
reference: IATI Standard v2.03; Internal Delegation of Authority Policy

description: Free-text field for additional context, internal commentary, or operational notes related to this amendment that do not fit structured fields. Used by grants management and partnership teams for internal coordination.
reference: Microsoft Dynamics 365 for Nonprofits — Partnership Agreement Module

description: The total authorized budget of the agreement prior to this amendment, expressed in the agreement currency. Retained for Budget versus Actual (BvA) analysis and audit trail of financial modifications.
reference: FASB ASC 958; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; Unit4 ERP — Grant Financial Management

business_glossary_term: Original Period of Performance End Date
description: The end date of the period of performance as defined in the agreement prior to this amendment. Used to calculate extension duration and track no-cost extension history.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.308; IATI Standard v2.03

business_glossary_term: Original Period of Performance Start Date
description: The start date of the period of performance as defined in the agreement prior to this amendment. Retained for audit trail and comparison purposes when a period-of-performance amendment is processed.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.308; IATI Standard v2.03

description: Full name of the authorized representative of the partner organization who signs this amendment on behalf of the CSO, CBO, INGO, government body, or UN agency. Required for signatory change amendments and legal enforceability.
reference: IATI Standard v2.03; CHS Alliance Core Humanitarian Standard

description: Updated total number of direct beneficiaries targeted under the agreement following this amendment. Null if beneficiary_target_change is False. Used for MEL/MEAL indicator tracking and donor results reporting.
reference: IATI Standard v2.03; SPHERE Standards; USAID ADS 303

description: The total authorized budget of the agreement as updated by this amendment. Null if this amendment does not modify the budget. Used for BvA reporting and donor financial compliance.
reference: FASB ASC 958; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; Unit4 ERP — Grant Financial Management

business_glossary_term: Revised Period of Performance End Date
description: The updated end date of the period of performance as established by this amendment. Null if this amendment does not modify the end date. Key field for no-cost extension tracking.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.308; IATI Standard v2.03

business_glossary_term: Revised Period of Performance Start Date
description: The updated start date of the period of performance as established by this amendment. Null if this amendment does not modify the start date.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.308; IATI Standard v2.03

description: Detailed narrative describing changes to the Scope of Work (SoW), program activities, geographic coverage, target beneficiary populations, or deliverables introduced by this amendment. Null if amendment type is not scope_change.
reference: USAID ADS 303; DFID Smart Rules; IATI Standard v2.03

description: The date on which the amendment request was formally submitted to the approval authority or donor/prime for review. Used to track processing timelines and compliance with advance notification requirements under 2 CFR 200.
System timestamp recording when this amendment record was last modified. Used for change tracking, data synchronization, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each partner capacity assessment record. Primary key for the capacity_assessment data product.
reference: USAID Organizational Capacity Assessment (OCA) Framework; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: Foreign key linking to partnership.partnership_agreement. Business justification: Capacity assessments are frequently conducted as a prerequisite or condition of a specific partnership agreement (e.g., required before a sub-award is executed). Linking capacity_assessment to the spe
Reference to the grant or funding agreement that triggered or is associated with this capacity assessment. Links due diligence records to specific funding instruments.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.331; USAID ADS 303maa

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.donor_requirement. Business justification: Donors frequently mandate partner capacity assessments as a specific donor requirement before disbursement. Linking the assessment to the triggering donor_requirement enables compliance officers to ve
Reference to the partner organization (CSO, CBO, INGO, government body, UN agency, or consortium) being assessed. Links to the partner master record.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Alliance; IATI Standard

description: Reference to the staff member or consultant who led the capacity assessment process. Used for accountability, quality assurance, and audit trail purposes.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 8; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: Reference to the immediately preceding capacity assessment record for the same partner, enabling longitudinal tracking of capacity development progress over time.
reference: USAID OCA Framework; DFID OCAT; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: Foreign key linking to grant.subaward. Business justification: Capacity assessments directly inform subaward risk ratings and payment modality decisions. This FK links the assessment to the specific subaward it informed, enabling subaward monitoring teams to retr
The principal date on which the capacity assessment was conducted or the field data collection was completed. Used as the primary business event date for reporting and due diligence timelines.
reference: USAID OCA Framework; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.331

description: Date on which the capacity assessment process was formally concluded and the final report was issued.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the capacity assessment was physically conducted. Relevant for field-based assessments of local partners.
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; IATI Standard v2.03; OCHA Geographic Reference

description: Standardized framework or tool used to conduct the capacity assessment. Common methodologies include USAID Organizational Capacity Assessment (OCA), DFID Organizational Capacity Assessment Tool (OCAT), ECHO Partner Assessment for Capacity Assessment (PACA), IRC Collaborative Capacity Assessment Tool (CCAT), and UNDP Capacity Scorecard. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USAID_OCA|DFID_OCAT|ECHO_PACA|IRC_CCAT|UNDP_CAPACITY_SCORECARD|OTHER — promote to reference product]
reference: USAID ADS 303; DFID Smart Rules; UNDP Capacity Development Framework

description: Externally-known alphanumeric reference code assigned to this capacity assessment, used in correspondence, donor reporting, and audit trails (e.g., NGO-CA-2024-000123).
reference: USAID OCA Framework; DFID Organizational Capacity Assessment Tool (OCAT); OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.331

description: URL or document management system path to the full capacity assessment report. Classified as confidential due to sensitive organizational information about the partner.
reference: USAID ADS 303; DFID Smart Rules; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: Indicates the depth and modality of the assessment: FULL (comprehensive on-site assessment covering all functional areas), ABBREVIATED (targeted review of selected areas), DESK_REVIEW (document-based review without site visit), FIELD_VISIT (on-site verification component).
reference: USAID ADS 303; DFID Smart Rules; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.331

description: Date on which the capacity assessment process formally commenced, including document review and stakeholder engagement initiation.

Current lifecycle state of the capacity assessment record: DRAFT (initiated but not submitted), IN_PROGRESS (assessment underway), COMPLETED (assessment finalized), VALIDATED (reviewed and approved by authorized staff), ARCHIVED (superseded or closed).
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Alliance; IATI Standard v2.03

description: Version number or edition of the assessment methodology tool used (e.g., 'USAID OCA v3.0', 'DFID OCAT 2019'). Ensures comparability of scores across assessments conducted using different tool versions.

Classification of when and why the assessment was conducted: PRE_AWARD (due diligence before partnership), PERIODIC (scheduled review during partnership), TRIGGERED (event-driven reassessment due to risk flag), or CLOSE_OUT (end-of-partnership evaluation).
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.331; USAID ADS 303maa; Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 8

description: Indicates whether the assessment results mandate the development of a formal capacity-building plan (True) or whether the partner's capacity is sufficient without a structured intervention plan (False).
reference: USAID ADS 303maa; Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 8; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.331

description: Timestamp indicating when the capacity assessment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting purposes.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; GDPR Article 5(2); FASB ASC 958

description: Indicates whether the assessment outcome requires enhanced programmatic or financial monitoring of the partner (True), such as more frequent reporting, additional site visits, or pre-approval of expenditures, as mandated by donor regulations.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.331; USAID ADS 303maa

description: Numeric score assigned to the partner's financial management functional area, covering accounting systems, internal controls, audit compliance, and fund stewardship. Typically scored on a 0–100 or 1–5 scale per the assessment methodology.
reference: USAID OCA Framework; DFID OCAT; FASB ASC 958; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.302

description: Specific risk rating for the partner's financial management functional area, used to determine sub-award monitoring intensity, payment modality, and required financial controls.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.331; USAID ADS 303; FASB ASC 958

description: Numeric score for the partner's organizational governance functional area, including board oversight, legal registration, organizational policies, and leadership accountability.
reference: USAID OCA Framework; DFID OCAT; Charity Commission (UK) Governance Code; IRS 501(c)(3) Regulations

description: Numeric score for the partner's human resources management functional area, including HR policies, recruitment practices, staff development, and safeguarding compliance.
reference: USAID OCA Framework; DFID OCAT; Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 8

description: Numeric score for the partner's ICT4D and information systems functional area, covering data management infrastructure, cybersecurity practices, and digital reporting capabilities.
reference: USAID OCA Framework; ICT4D Principles for Digital Development

description: Concise narrative summary of the principal findings from the capacity assessment, highlighting major strengths, critical gaps, and priority areas for improvement across all assessed functional areas.
reference: USAID OCA Framework; DFID OCAT; DAC Evaluation Criteria

description: Unique submission identifier from KoboToolbox or similar field data collection platform used to capture assessment data in the field. Enables traceability back to the source data collection record.
reference: KoboToolbox Data Collection Platform; ICT4D Principles for Digital Development

description: Full name of the individual who led the capacity assessment. Retained for reporting and audit purposes even when the staff record may change.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; USAID ADS 303

description: Name of the organization to which the lead assessor belongs (e.g., the NGO's headquarters, an independent consulting firm, or a donor agency). Relevant when external assessors are engaged.

business_glossary_term: Monitoring Evaluation and Learning (MEL) Capacity Score
description: Numeric score for the partner's Monitoring, Evaluation, and Learning (MEL) functional area, covering data collection systems, indicator tracking, reporting quality, and learning practices.
reference: USAID ADS 201; DFID Smart Rules; IATI Standard v2.03; DAC Evaluation Criteria

description: Scheduled date by which the next capacity assessment must be completed, based on risk rating, donor requirements, or organizational policy. Drives compliance monitoring and workflow triggers.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.331; USAID ADS 303maa

description: Categorical risk classification assigned to the partner based on the capacity assessment results: LOW (strong capacity, minimal oversight needed), MEDIUM (adequate capacity with some gaps), HIGH (significant capacity gaps requiring intensive support), CRITICAL (fundamental weaknesses posing fiduciary or programmatic risk).
reference: USAID ADS 303maa; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.331; Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 8

description: Composite numeric score representing the partner's overall organizational capacity across all assessed functional areas. Calculated per the assessment methodology's weighting scheme and recorded as the official assessment result.
reference: USAID OCA Framework; DFID OCAT; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.331

description: Indicates whether the partner organization completed a self-assessment component prior to or alongside the external assessment (True). Many OCA methodologies include a parallel self-assessment to identify perception gaps.
reference: USAID OCA Framework; DFID OCAT; HAP (Humanitarian Accountability Partnership) Standard

description: Overall score submitted by the partner organization in their self-assessment component. Compared against the external assessor's score to identify perception gaps and inform capacity-building dialogue.

Payment modality recommended for sub-award disbursements based on the partner's financial risk rating: ADVANCE (funds transferred before expenditure), REIMBURSEMENT (funds transferred after expenditure documentation), DIRECT_PAYMENT (NGO pays vendors directly on partner's behalf), MIXED (combination of modalities).
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.305; USAID ADS 303maa

description: Numeric score for the partner's procurement and supply chain management functional area, covering procurement policies, competitive bidding, vendor management, and asset control.
reference: USAID OCA Framework; DFID OCAT; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.317–200.326

description: Numeric score for the partner's program design and management functional area, including project planning, implementation capacity, beneficiary targeting, and service delivery quality.
reference: USAID OCA Framework; DFID OCAT; Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitments 1–9

description: Narrative description of the specific capacity-building interventions recommended based on assessment findings, such as financial management training, procurement system upgrades, MEL system strengthening, or governance policy development.
reference: USAID ADS 303maa; DFID Smart Rules; Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 8

description: The maximum possible score on the scoring scale used by the assessment methodology (e.g., 100 for percentage-based scales, 5 for Likert-based scales). Required to normalize scores across different methodologies for comparative analytics.
Timestamp indicating when the capacity assessment record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, data synchronization, and audit trail purposes.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; GDPR Article 5(2)

description: Date on which the capacity assessment was formally reviewed and validated by the authorized staff member, marking the record as officially approved for use in partnership and sub-award decisions.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; USAID ADS 303

description: Unique system-generated identifier for the capacity building plan record. Primary key for this entity. Role: MASTER_AGREEMENT — this product represents a structured, time-bound plan (agreement-like container) governing capacity-building interventions for a partner organization.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Alliance; Grand Bargain Localization Workstream

description: Foreign key linking to partnership.partnership_agreement. Business justification: A capacity building plan is typically developed as a requirement or condition of a specific partnership agreement (e.g., a sub-award agreement may mandate capacity strengthening). Linking capacity_bui
Reference to the grant or sub-award under which this capacity building plan is funded and implemented. Links to the grant master record in the grant domain.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; IATI Standard

description: Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Capacity building plans require formal budget allocation for training, technical assistance, and organizational development activities. Links plan to approved budget for expenditure tracking, variance

Reference to the formal capacity assessment record that identified the gaps and informed the design of this capacity building plan. Ensures traceability from assessment findings to planned interventions.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Alliance; USAID Organizational Capacity Assessment (OCA) Framework

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.corrective_action_plan. Business justification: Capacity building plans are directly created in response to corrective action plans when audit findings or compliance incidents identify partner capability gaps (e.g., a CAP finding of weak financial

Foreign key linking to compliance.donor_requirement. Business justification: Donors frequently require capacity building plans as a condition of sub-awards to local NGO partners (Grand Bargain localization commitments, USAID partner capacity requirements). Linking capacity_bui

Reference to the partner organization (Civil Society Organization, Community-Based Organization, or local INGO) for whom this capacity building plan has been developed. Links to the partner organization master record in the partnership domain.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Alliance; Grand Bargain Localization Workstream

description: Foreign key linking to partnership.partner_performance_review. Business justification: A capacity building plan is frequently triggered by findings from a partner performance review (e.g., when capacity_building_recommended = TRUE on the review). Linking the plan back to the triggering
Reference to the Ngo staff member (e.g., Partnership Officer, Capacity Building Advisor) who is the primary owner and accountable lead for this plan's implementation.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Alliance; RACI Framework

description: Date on which this capacity building plan was formally approved by the designated authority at Ngo, authorizing commencement of activities and budget expenditure.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Alliance; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: Name or identifier of the Ngo staff member or management authority who formally approved this capacity building plan for implementation. Supports governance and accountability documentation.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Alliance; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: Numeric score representing the partner organization's overall organizational capacity at the time of the baseline assessment, prior to plan implementation. Typically scored on a standardized scale (e.g., 1–5 or 0–100) per the assessment tool used.
reference: USAID Organizational Capacity Assessment (OCA) Framework; Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Alliance

description: The scoring scale used for baseline, target, and current capacity scores in this plan (e.g., '1_to_5', '0_to_100'). Ensures consistent interpretation of scores across different assessment tools and reporting periods.
reference: USAID Organizational Capacity Assessment (OCA) Framework; Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Alliance

description: Indicates whether this capacity building plan is designed in alignment with the Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) on Quality and Accountability, ensuring the partner is supported to meet CHS commitments.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the partner organization operates and where this capacity building plan is being implemented (e.g., 'SOM', 'SDN', 'ETH').
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; IATI Standard v2.03; OCHA Geographic Reference

description: Timestamp indicating when this capacity building plan record was first created in the system. Corresponds to the MASTER_AGREEMENT RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: FASB ASC 958 (Not-for-Profit Entities); OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the local currency in which the plan budget is also tracked (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'KES'). Supports multi-currency financial reporting for field operations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IATI Standard v2.03

description: Most recently recorded organizational capacity score for the partner, updated following periodic reassessments or milestone reviews during plan implementation. Enables tracking of progress toward the target capacity score.
reference: USAID Organizational Capacity Assessment (OCA) Framework; MEL (Monitoring Evaluation and Learning) Framework

description: Indicates whether progress on this capacity building plan must be reported to the donor as part of grant compliance obligations. True = donor reporting required; False = internal plan only.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; USAID Bureau for Humanitarian Assistance (BHA) Reporting Requirements

description: The planned or actual date on which the capacity building plan concludes and all activities are expected to be completed. Nullable for open-ended plans. Corresponds to the MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_UNTIL category.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; IATI Standard v2.03

description: Cumulative actual expenditure incurred against this capacity building plan to date, expressed in US Dollars (USD). Used for Budget versus Actual (BvA) tracking and financial stewardship reporting.
reference: FASB ASC 958 (Not-for-Profit Entities); OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; Unit4 ERP — Grant Financial Management

description: Indicates whether gender mainstreaming and Gender-Based Violence (GBV) prevention components are explicitly included in this capacity building plan, in line with donor gender policies and SDG (Sustainable Development Goal) 5 commitments.
reference: USAID Gender Equality and Female Empowerment Policy; SDG 5; Inter-Agency Standing Committee (IASC) Gender Handbook

description: Reference code or description of the specific Grand Bargain commitment (e.g., Workstream 2 — Localization) that this capacity building plan contributes to. Supports donor accountability and IATI transparency reporting.
reference: Grand Bargain Localization Workstream; IATI Standard v2.03

description: Sub-national region, state, or province where the partner organization and this capacity building plan are primarily based. Supports geographic disaggregation in MEL dashboards and GIS (Geographic Information System) reporting.
reference: OCHA Geographic Reference; IATI Standard v2.03

description: Date of the most recent formal review or progress assessment of this capacity building plan, during which activity completion, capacity scores, and budget utilization were evaluated.
reference: MEL (Monitoring Evaluation and Learning) Framework; Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Alliance

description: Indicates whether this capacity building plan is formally aligned with Ngo's localization strategy and Grand Bargain commitments to strengthen local and national actors. True = aligned and documented; False = not formally aligned.
reference: Grand Bargain Localization Workstream; Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Alliance

description: Date on which the next formal review of this capacity building plan is scheduled. Drives workflow reminders and ensures timely monitoring of plan progress.
reference: MEL (Monitoring Evaluation and Learning) Framework; Microsoft Dynamics 365 for Nonprofits — Program Management Module

description: Free-text field for additional contextual notes, observations, or qualitative commentary on the capacity building plan, including lessons learned, contextual challenges, or coordination notes with OCHA cluster leads.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Alliance; MEAL (Monitoring Evaluation Accountability and Learning) Framework

description: High-level statement of the primary objective of this capacity building plan, describing the intended organizational capability improvement to be achieved by the plan's end date.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Alliance; USAID Organizational Capacity Assessment (OCA) Framework

description: Qualitative assessment of the overall implementation progress of the capacity building plan relative to the planned timeline and milestones. Distinct from plan_status (lifecycle state); this reflects delivery performance.
reference: MEL (Monitoring Evaluation and Learning) Framework; Grand Bargain Localization Workstream

description: Full name of the designated focal point or primary contact person at the partner organization who is responsible for coordinating and participating in capacity building activities under this plan.
reference: GDPR Article 4; Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Alliance

description: Job title or role of the partner organization's designated focal point for this capacity building plan (e.g., 'Executive Director', 'Finance Manager', 'MEL Officer').
Externally-known alphanumeric reference code assigned to this capacity building plan, used in donor reports, partner correspondence, and IATI disclosures. Format: CBP-[PartnerCode]-[Year]-[Sequence].
reference: IATI Standard v2.03; Grand Bargain Localization Workstream

description: Current lifecycle status of the capacity building plan. 'draft' = under development; 'active' = approved and in implementation; 'on_hold' = temporarily suspended; 'completed' = all activities delivered and closed; 'cancelled' = plan discontinued.
reference: Microsoft Dynamics 365 for Nonprofits — Program Management Module; Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Alliance

description: Human-readable title of the capacity building plan, typically referencing the partner organization name and the primary functional area targeted (e.g., 'Financial Management Strengthening Plan — Partner XYZ 2024').
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Alliance; Grand Bargain Localization Workstream

description: Classification of the primary focus area of the capacity building plan. 'organizational' = overall organizational development; 'technical' = sector-specific technical skills; 'financial' = financial management and accountability; 'governance' = board and leadership strengthening; 'mel' = Monitoring, Evaluation, and Learning (MEL) systems. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: organizational|technical|financial|governance|mel|procurement|hr|communications|safeguarding — promote to reference product]
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Alliance; USAID Organizational Capacity Assessment (OCA) Framework

description: Frequency at which progress updates on this capacity building plan must be reported internally or to the donor. Drives scheduling of review meetings and SitRep (Situation Report) contributions.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; USAID Bureau for Humanitarian Assistance (BHA) Reporting Requirements

description: Indicates whether this capacity building plan includes a dedicated safeguarding (Protection from Sexual Exploitation and Abuse — PSEA) component. Required for compliance with donor safeguarding policies and CHS commitments.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Alliance; USAID Safeguarding Policy; Inter-Agency Standing Committee (IASC) PSEA Guidelines

description: The date on which the capacity building plan officially commences and activities are scheduled to begin. Corresponds to the MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_FROM category.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; IATI Standard v2.03

description: Numeric score representing the desired organizational capacity level to be achieved by the partner organization upon successful completion of this plan. Used to measure progress and plan effectiveness.
reference: USAID Organizational Capacity Assessment (OCA) Framework; Results-Based Management (RBM) Framework

description: Comma-separated list of organizational functional areas targeted by this plan for strengthening, as identified in the capacity assessment. Common values include: financial management, human resources, procurement, governance, MEL (Monitoring Evaluation and Learning), safeguarding, communications, ICT4D (Information and Communication Technology for Development).
reference: USAID Organizational Capacity Assessment (OCA) Framework; Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Alliance

description: Narrative summary of the Theory of Change (ToC) underpinning this capacity building plan, describing the causal pathway from planned interventions to expected organizational capability improvements and ultimate program outcomes.
reference: OECD DAC (Development Assistance Committee) Evaluation Criteria; Grand Bargain Localization Workstream

description: Count of capacity building activity line-items that have been fully delivered and marked as completed under this plan. Used to calculate overall plan completion rate for MEL dashboards and donor reporting.
reference: Microsoft Dynamics 365 for Nonprofits — Program Management Module; MEL (Monitoring Evaluation and Learning) Framework

description: Total number of capacity building activity line-items (trainings, mentoring sessions, technical assistance visits, peer learning events) planned under this plan. Used for progress tracking and workload planning.
reference: Microsoft Dynamics 365 for Nonprofits — Program Management Module

description: Total approved budget allocated for the implementation of this capacity building plan, expressed in US Dollars (USD). Covers all activity costs including training, technical assistance, materials, and facilitator fees.
reference: FASB ASC 958 (Not-for-Profit Entities); OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; Unit4 ERP — Grant Financial Management

description: Timestamp indicating when this capacity building plan record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: FASB ASC 958 (Not-for-Profit Entities); OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each partner performance review record. Primary key for the partner_performance_review data product.
Reference to the governing agreement (MoU, LoA, SoW, or sub-award) under which this performance review is conducted.
reference: Microsoft Dynamics 365 for Nonprofits — Agreement Record

description: Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Partner performance reviews assess budget_utilisation_rate as a core financial performance indicator. NGO program managers need the linked budget record to compute accurate utilization scores and prod

Foreign key linking to partnership.capacity_assessment. Business justification: A partner performance review should reference the capacity assessment that was in effect during the review period, providing the capability baseline against which performance is evaluated. This is a d

Foreign key linking to compliance.donor_requirement. Business justification: Donors routinely require periodic partner performance reviews as a named condition of grant agreements. Linking partner_performance_review to the specific donor_requirement it satisfies enables compli
Reference to the program or project that the implementing partner is delivering under review.
reference: Microsoft Dynamics 365 for Nonprofits — Program Record

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.internal_review. Business justification: Partner performance reviews may trigger or reference internal compliance reviews when issues are identified. Links performance monitoring to compliance oversight. Essential for integrated risk managem
Reference to the implementing partner organisation being reviewed. Links to the partner master record in the partnership domain.
reference: Microsoft Dynamics 365 for Nonprofits — Partner/Account Record

description: Reference to the staff member or team lead who conducted and is accountable for this performance review.
Date on which the performance review was formally approved by the authorised reviewer or partnership manager.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; Internal Governance Policy

description: Narrative description of performance gaps, weaknesses, or risks identified during the review that require corrective action or capacity strengthening.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Alliance; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: Indicates whether beneficiary feedback (e.g., from Post-Distribution Monitoring (PDM), Focus Group Discussions (FGD), or KoboToolbox surveys) was incorporated into the review findings.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Alliance — Commitment 4 (Participation); HAP Standard

description: Percentage of the sub-award budget expended by the partner during the review period (0.00–100.00). Key Budget versus Actual (BvA) indicator for financial stewardship assessment.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; FASB ASC 958; Unit4 ERP — Grant Financial Management

description: Narrative description of specific capacity building areas recommended for the partner (e.g., financial management, MEL systems, safeguarding, ICT4D). Populated when capacity_building_recommended is True.
reference: USAID Local Capacity Strengthening Policy; Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Alliance

description: Indicates whether the reviewer recommends a formal capacity building or organisational development intervention for the partner based on identified gaps.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Alliance; USAID Local Capacity Strengthening Policy

description: Detailed description of required corrective actions, including specific steps the partner must take to address identified deficiencies. Populated when corrective_action_required is True.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Alliance

description: Deadline by which the partner must demonstrate completion of required corrective actions. Null when corrective_action_required is False.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Alliance

description: Indicates whether a formal Corrective Action Plan (CAP) is required as an outcome of this review. True triggers a CAP workflow in the partnership management system.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 — Subrecipient Monitoring; Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Alliance

description: Current status of the corrective action plan associated with this review. Tracks partner remediation progress for risk management and compliance reporting.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Alliance

description: Timestamp when this performance review record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.
reference: FASB ASC 958; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 — Record Retention

description: Indicates whether a physical field visit to the partner's implementation site was conducted as part of this review. Required for high-risk partners per OMB Uniform Guidance subrecipient monitoring standards.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 — Subrecipient Monitoring
