description: Primary key for audit_finding
Reference to the specific grant or award under which this finding was identified, if applicable.
Reference to the corrective action plan developed to address this finding. Links to the detailed remediation plan with responsible parties, timelines, and action steps.

Foreign key linking to field.country_office. Business justification: Audit findings are associated with specific country offices for remediation assignment and tracking. Linking audit_finding to country_office enables compliance teams to generate office-level audit fin
Reference to the federal program under which this finding was identified, if applicable to a specific program.

Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Audit findings are attributed to specific org units for corrective action ownership and organizational risk reporting. responsible_department on audit_finding is a denormalized plain-text reference to

Foreign key linking to compliance.single_audit. Business justification: Audit findings discovered during Single Audit engagements (OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200) should reference the specific Single Audit. The existing audit_id points to safeguarding.audit (cross-domain)

Foreign key linking to grant.subaward. Business justification: Federal Single Audit findings under 2 CFR 200 are often specific to individual subawards (questioned costs, compliance failures at subawardee level). Linking audit_finding to the specific subaward ena
The actual date on which the finding was fully resolved and remediated, as verified by management or subsequent audit. Null if the finding is still open or in remediation.
The end date of the audit period during which this finding was identified. Typically the end of the organization's fiscal year under audit.
The start date of the audit period during which this finding was identified. Typically the beginning of the organization's fiscal year under audit.
Name of the external audit firm or auditor who identified this finding during the audit engagement.
Explanation of the underlying reason or root cause that led to the condition. This identifies why the deficiency occurred (e.g., lack of training, inadequate controls, resource constraints).

business_glossary_term: Catalog of Federal Domestic Assistance (CFDA) Number
description: The five-digit CFDA number (now known as Assistance Listings number) identifying the federal program under which the finding was identified. Format is XX.XXX where the first two digits represent the federal agency and the last three represent the specific program.
reference: 2 CFR 200.331 Requirements for Pass-through Entities; SAM.gov Assistance Listings

description: The specific type of compliance requirement that was violated or not met, as defined in the OMB Compliance Supplement (e.g., Activities Allowed or Unallowed, Allowable Costs/Cost Principles, Cash Management, Eligibility, Equipment and Real Property Management, Matching, Period of Performance, Procurement, Program Income, Reporting, Subrecipient Monitoring, Special Tests and Provisions).
Detailed description of the actual condition or deficiency found during the audit. This describes what the auditor observed or identified as problematic.
The timestamp when this audit finding record was first created in the system.
reference: Data Governance Framework - Audit Trail Standards

description: Description of the required standard, regulation, policy, or grant provision that was not met. This establishes the benchmark against which the condition is measured.

Description of the actual or potential impact or consequence of the finding. This explains what harm or risk resulted from the condition (e.g., misstated financial statements, unallowable costs charged to grant, noncompliance with donor restrictions).
The target date by which the organization expects to fully resolve and remediate this finding based on the corrective action plan.
The date on which the audit report containing this finding was submitted to the Federal Audit Clearinghouse. Null if not yet submitted.
Name of the federal agency that provided the funding for the program under which this finding was identified (e.g., USAID, BHA, Department of State, HHS).
The unique Federal Award Identification Number assigned by the federal agency to the specific award under which this finding was identified.
The date on which the auditor formally identified and documented this finding during the audit fieldwork or reporting process.
The externally-known unique reference number assigned to this audit finding by the auditor, typically following the format YYYY-NNN or similar audit-specific numbering convention.

Current lifecycle status of the audit finding. Open indicates newly identified and not yet addressed. In remediation indicates corrective action plan is being implemented. Resolved indicates corrective actions completed and pending verification. Closed indicates finding fully remediated and verified. Repeat finding indicates this issue was identified in a prior audit period.
Brief descriptive title or summary of the audit finding, providing a high-level overview of the issue identified.

Classification of the audit finding based on severity and nature. Material weakness indicates a deficiency or combination of deficiencies in internal control such that there is a reasonable possibility that a material misstatement will not be prevented or detected. Significant deficiency is less severe than a material weakness but important enough to merit attention. Questioned cost represents costs that do not comply with grant terms. Noncompliance indicates violation of laws, regulations, or grant provisions.
reference: 2 CFR 200.516 Audit Findings; Government Auditing Standards (Yellow Book)

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this finding includes indicators of potential fraud, waste, or abuse. True if fraud indicators are present and require further investigation.

Specific nature of restrictions placed on the fund by donors or the board. Governs allowable expenditures and compliance requirements under OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.403 - Factors Affecting Allowability

description: Comma-separated list or description of cost categories that are explicitly unallowable for this fund. Prevents non-compliant expense posting.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.420-475 - Unallowable Costs

description: Primary key for fiscal_period
Reference to the corresponding fiscal period in the prior fiscal year. Enables year-over-year comparative analysis.
Indicates whether this fiscal period requires external audit review, typically for annual periods or grant compliance.
The date when the fiscal period was officially closed. Nullable if period is still open or future.
Timestamp when this fiscal period record was first created in the system.
Total number of calendar days in the fiscal period. Used for prorations, accruals, and time-weighted calculations.
The last day of the fiscal period. Defines when the period closes for financial transactions and reporting.
The date for which exchange rates are locked for multi-currency translation in this fiscal period. Nullable for single-currency organizations.
Detailed description or notes about the fiscal period, including any special considerations, adjustments, or organizational context.
The fiscal year to which this period belongs (e.g., 2024). Supports non-calendar fiscal years common in nonprofit organizations.
Indicates whether this fiscal period record is currently active and available for use in financial transactions and reporting.
Indicates whether this is a special adjustment period (typically Period 13) used for year-end closing entries and corrections.
Indicates whether this is the final period of the fiscal year, requiring special year-end closing procedures and financial statement preparation.
The date when the fiscal period was permanently locked, preventing any further modifications. Nullable if not yet locked.
Timestamp when this fiscal period record was last modified. Updated whenever period status or attributes change.
The date when the fiscal period was officially opened for transaction posting. May differ from start_date due to administrative processes.
Short alphanumeric code representing the fiscal period (e.g., FY24-Q1, P01-2024). Used as a business identifier for reporting and reference.
Human-readable name of the fiscal period (e.g., 'January 2024', 'Q1 FY2024', 'Period 1 - FY2024').
Sequential number of the period within the fiscal year (e.g., 1 for January, 4 for Q1, 12 for December). Used for ordering and reporting.
Current lifecycle status of the fiscal period. Open allows transactions; closed prevents new entries but allows adjustments; locked is final and immutable; future indicates not yet active.
Classification of the fiscal period by duration and frequency (monthly, quarterly, semi-annual, annual, or custom).
The fiscal quarter number (1-4) to which this period belongs. Nullable for non-quarterly periods.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for financial reporting in this period (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency nonprofit operations.
The first day of the fiscal period. Defines when the period becomes effective for financial transactions and reporting.
Number of business working days in the fiscal period, excluding weekends and holidays. Used for operational metrics and staffing calculations.
Unique identifier for the general ledger account. Primary key for the GL account master data.
Detailed business description of the GL account purpose, usage guidelines, and what types of transactions should be posted to this account.
The account group or category within the Chart of Accounts structure (e.g., 'Current Assets', 'Fixed Assets', 'Restricted Contributions', 'Program Expenses').
The human-readable descriptive name of the GL account (e.g., 'Cash - Operating Account', 'Grant Revenue - USAID', 'Program Salaries - WASH').

The externally-known unique account number within the Chart of Accounts (CoA) structure. This is the business identifier used in SAP S/4HANA and Unit4 ERP for all financial transactions and reporting.
reference: FASB ASC 958 - Not-for-Profit Entities, GAAP for Nonprofits

description: The name or identifier of the business owner or responsible party for this GL account (e.g., Finance Director, Program Manager, Grant Manager).
The current lifecycle status of the GL account: active (available for posting), inactive (temporarily suspended), blocked (posting prohibited), or closed (permanently retired).
reference: SAP S/4HANA Financial Accounting, Unit4 ERP

description: The fundamental accounting classification of the GL account: asset, liability, net asset (equity for nonprofits per FASB ASC 958), revenue, or expense.
reference: FASB ASC 958 - Not-for-Profit Entities, GAAP for Nonprofits

description: Indicates whether this account is subject to budget control and Budget versus Actual (BvA) monitoring. When true, transactions require budget availability checking before posting.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200, Grant Management Best Practices

description: The Chart of Accounts structure identifier or version code. Nonprofits may maintain multiple CoA structures for different legal entities or reporting requirements.
The GL account number used for consolidated financial reporting across multiple legal entities or country offices. Used when the nonprofit has a multi-entity structure.
Indicates whether this account serves as an allocation base for distributing indirect costs across programs and grants (e.g., direct salaries, total direct costs).
The date and time when this GL account record was first created in the system.
The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for this GL account (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Nonprofits operating internationally may maintain accounts in multiple currencies.
The OECD Development Assistance Committee sector classification code for international development activities. Required for International Aid Transparency Initiative (IATI) reporting.
reference: OECD DAC, International Aid Transparency Initiative (IATI)

description: Indicates whether this account tracks donor-restricted funds requiring specific usage compliance and separate reporting per donor agreements and FASB ASC 958.
The date when this GL account was closed or became inactive. Null for currently active accounts.
The date when this GL account became active and available for transaction posting in the Chart of Accounts.
The mapping code used for external regulatory reporting (e.g., IRS Form 990 line item, IATI activity code, donor-specific reporting category).
reference: IRS Form 990, International Aid Transparency Initiative (IATI)

description: The primary financial statement where this account appears: balance sheet (statement of financial position), statement of activities (income statement equivalent for nonprofits), statement of functional expenses, statement of cash flows, or off-balance-sheet.
reference: FASB ASC 958 - Not-for-Profit Entities, GAAP for Nonprofits

description: The functional expense classification required by FASB ASC 958 for expense accounts: program services, management and general (administrative), fundraising, or not applicable (for non-expense accounts). Used for Form 990 reporting and functional expense allocation.
reference: FASB ASC 958 - Not-for-Profit Entities, IRS Form 990

description: The net asset restriction classification per FASB ASC 958: unrestricted (no donor restrictions), temporarily restricted (time or purpose restrictions), or permanently restricted (endowment principal). Critical for nonprofit fund accounting and donor compliance.
Indicates whether expenses posted to this account are eligible for reimbursement under federal grants per OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200. Critical for grant financial management and cost allocation.
The level of this account within the Chart of Accounts hierarchy (e.g., 1 for top-level summary accounts, 2 for sub-accounts, 3 for detail posting accounts).
Indicates whether this account is part of the indirect cost pool for Negotiated Indirect Cost Rate Agreement (NICRA) calculations and Facilities and Administration (F&A) cost allocation.
The date and time when this GL account record was most recently updated.
The date of the most recent transaction posted to this GL account. Used for account activity monitoring and dormant account identification.

The natural classification of expense by type (e.g., salaries, benefits, travel, supplies, professional fees, rent, utilities). Used alongside functional classification for comprehensive expense reporting per FASB ASC 958.
Indicates whether this account normally carries a debit or credit balance per double-entry accounting principles.
Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this GL account usage, restrictions, or compliance requirements.
Indicates whether transactions can be directly posted to this account (true for detail accounts) or if it is a summary/roll-up account that only receives aggregated balances (false).
reference: SAP S/4HANA Financial Accounting, Unit4 ERP

description: The program or service line code that this account supports (e.g., WASH, Health, Education, Emergency Response). Used for program-level financial reporting and Monitoring Evaluation and Learning (MEL).
reference: FASB ASC 958 - Not-for-Profit Entities, IRS Form 990

description: Indicates whether this account requires regular reconciliation (e.g., bank accounts, accounts receivable, accounts payable control accounts). Used for internal control and audit compliance.
reference: GAAP for Nonprofits, Internal Control Standards

description: The United Nations Sustainable Development Goal(s) that activities posted to this account contribute toward (e.g., SDG 1 - No Poverty, SDG 6 - Clean Water and Sanitation). Used for impact reporting.
Indicates the tax treatment of transactions posted to this account: taxable income, non-taxable, tax-exempt per IRS 501(c)(3) regulations, or not applicable.
Indicates whether revenue posted to this account constitutes Unrelated Business Income (UBI) subject to taxation under IRS regulations for tax-exempt organizations.
Unique identifier for the journal entry record. Primary key for the journal entry entity.

Foreign key reference to the grant or award that this journal entry is financially associated with. Critical for grant-specific financial tracking, budget versus actual reporting, and donor reporting compliance.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 - Grant Financial Management

description: Foreign key linking to finance.bank_account. Business justification: journal_entry has no bank_account_id FK. Bank-related journal entries (bank reconciliation adjustments, bank charges, interest income) must reference the specific bank account they affect. Adding bank
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Journal entries are allocated to cost centers at the header level. This FK enables cost center reporting and management analysis.

Foreign key linking to field.country_office. Business justification: Journal entries are often specific to a country office. Finance needs country-level expense tracking for management reporting, donor reporting, and HQ consolidation.

business_glossary_term: Created By Staff Member Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to workforce.staff_member. Business justification: Finance tracks which staff member created each journal entry for audit trail and accountability - fundamental accounting control requirement in nonprofit financial management.

Foreign key linking to field.deployment. Business justification: Deployment costs are recorded via journal entries. Finance needs to link JEs to deployments for cost tracking, audit trail, and deployment cost analysis.
Foreign key linking to field.team. Business justification: Team operating costs are recorded via journal entries. Finance needs to link JEs to teams for team budget tracking and team cost analysis.

Foreign key linking to finance.finance_fund. Business justification: Journal entries are allocated to funds at the header level. This FK enables fund-level reporting and ensures compliance with donor restrictions.

Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Journal entries are posted to specific fiscal periods for financial reporting and period close processes. The existing fiscal_year (INT) and fiscal_period (INT) columns are denormalized representation
Foreign key reference to the program or project that this journal entry supports. Enables program-level cost tracking, budget monitoring, and Results-Based Management (RBM) financial reporting.

Foreign key linking to field.project_site. Business justification: Many journal entries relate to specific field sites (site operating costs, site setup costs). Finance needs to track expenses by site for management reporting and site cost analysis.
Date and time when this journal entry was approved for posting. Null if not yet approved. Part of the audit trail for approval workflow compliance.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 - Internal Controls

description: Additional free-text notes or comments added for audit trail purposes, explaining unusual circumstances, supporting documentation references, or special handling instructions.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 - Documentation Requirements

description: Batch identifier grouping multiple journal entries that were posted together as a single batch. Used for batch-level approval workflows and mass reversal operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA for Nonprofits - Batch Processing Standards

description: Legal entity or company code within the ERP system to which this journal entry belongs. Essential for multi-entity nonprofit organizations with separate legal entities or country offices.
reference: SAP S/4HANA for Nonprofits - Organizational Structure

description: Compliance status flag indicating whether this journal entry has been reviewed for regulatory compliance (OMB Uniform Guidance, FASB ASC 958, donor restrictions). Used for compliance monitoring and audit preparation.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 - Compliance Requirements

description: Date and time when this journal entry record was first created in the system. Foundational audit timestamp for record lifecycle tracking.
reference: Data Governance Framework - Audit Trail Standards

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code indicating the transaction currency of this journal entry (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Essential for multi-currency grant accounting and foreign exchange management.
The date the journal entry document was created or originated, which may differ from the posting date. Used for audit trail and document sequencing.
The externally-known unique document number assigned to this journal entry in the ERP system (SAP S/4HANA or Unit4). Used for audit trails and cross-system reconciliation.
reference: GAAP for Nonprofits - Financial Reporting Standards

description: Classification of the journal entry document type indicating the nature of the posting: original entry, reversal of prior entry, accrual for period-end, cost allocation, reclassification between accounts, or manual adjustment.
Detailed narrative description of the business purpose and nature of this journal entry. Provides context for financial reviewers, auditors, and future reference.
reference: GAAP for Nonprofits - Financial Reporting Standards

description: The foreign exchange rate applied to convert transaction currency amounts to the organization's functional currency (typically USD). Null for entries in functional currency. Critical for multi-currency grant financial management.

Functional area classification (e.g., program services, management and general, fundraising) required for nonprofit functional expense reporting per FASB ASC 958. Supports Form 990 and donor reporting.
Three-letter ISO 4217 code for the organization's functional reporting currency. All journal entries are ultimately reported in this currency for consolidated financial statements.

The Negotiated Indirect Cost Rate Agreement (NICRA) rate applied to this journal entry for facilities and administration (F&A) cost allocation. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1500 for 15%). Critical for federal grant compliance.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 - Indirect Cost Rates

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this journal entry is a manual adjustment or correction entry (as opposed to a system-generated or routine operational entry). True for adjustments; false for standard entries.
reference: GAAP for Nonprofits - Financial Reporting Standards

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this journal entry represents an inter-company or inter-entity transaction requiring elimination in consolidated financial statements. True for inter-company entries; false otherwise.

Ledger group or ledger type identifier (e.g., leading ledger, extension ledger, parallel accounting ledger) used in systems supporting multiple parallel accounting views. Supports GAAP and IFRS parallel reporting.
Date and time when this journal entry was last modified. Null if never modified. Critical for audit trail and change history tracking.
reference: Data Governance Framework - Audit Trail Standards

description: The business date on which this journal entry was posted to the general ledger. This is the effective date for financial reporting and determines which fiscal period the entry impacts.
reference: GAAP for Nonprofits - Financial Reporting Standards

description: System-specific posting key code (from SAP or Unit4) that determines the account type (debit/credit) and controls which fields are required during entry. Technical control field for ERP posting logic.
reference: SAP S/4HANA for Nonprofits - Configuration Standards

description: Current lifecycle status of the journal entry in the approval and posting workflow. Tracks progression from draft creation through approval to final posting in the general ledger.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 - Internal Controls

description: External reference number linking this journal entry to source documents such as invoices, payment vouchers, grant agreements, or other originating transactions. Supports audit trail and source document traceability.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 - Documentation Requirements

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this journal entry is a reversal of a previously posted entry. True if this entry reverses another entry; false otherwise.
reference: GAAP for Nonprofits - Financial Reporting Standards

description: Free-text explanation of why this journal entry was reversed. Required for audit compliance and internal control documentation when reversal_indicator is true.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 - Internal Controls

description: The document number of the original journal entry that this entry reverses. Populated only when reversal_indicator is true. Maintains bidirectional audit trail for reversals.

Identifier of the system or interface that originated this journal entry. Distinguishes between entries created directly in the ERP (SAP S/4HANA, Unit4), manual entries, or entries interfaced from subsidiary systems.
reference: Data Governance Framework - Source System Tracking

description: Unique identifier for the journal entry line item. Primary key for the journal entry line product.
Reference to the grant or award that this line item is charged to. Enables grant financial management and compliance reporting per OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200, FASB ASC 958

description: Reference to the budget line item against which this actual expenditure or revenue is tracked. Enables Budget versus Actual (BvA) variance analysis.
reference: Management Accounting Standards, OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Journal entry lines are allocated to cost centers for expense tracking and management reporting. This FK enables joining to get cost center hierarchy, manager, and program area.

Foreign key linking to finance.finance_fund. Business justification: Journal entry lines are allocated to funds for fund accounting and donor restriction tracking. This FK enables joining to get fund type, restriction type, and donor information.

Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: journal_entry_line has fiscal_period (INT) and fiscal_year (INT) as raw scalar fields rather than a proper FK to fiscal_period. Adding fiscal_period_id normalizes this to reference the authoritative f

Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Every journal entry line posts to a GL account. This is the core relationship for the general ledger. The FK enables joining to get account type, financial statement classification, and CoA hierarchy.
Reference to the parent journal entry header that contains this line item. Links the line to its transaction header.
Reference to the original journal entry line ID that this line reverses or corrects. Null if this is not a reversal. Supports audit trail and error correction tracking.
Reference to the vendor or supplier associated with this line item for accounts payable transactions. Supports vendor payment tracking and procurement compliance.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200, Procurement Standards

description: Boolean indicator of whether this cost is allowable under the applicable grant or award terms per OMB Uniform Guidance. Used for grant financial compliance.
The approval status of this journal entry line within the financial workflow. Tracks whether the line has been reviewed, approved, and posted to the GL.
reference: Internal Controls Standards, GAAP for Nonprofits

description: The date and time when this journal entry line was approved. Supports audit trails and compliance reporting.
The user ID or name of the person who approved this journal entry line. Supports internal controls and audit trails per OMB Uniform Guidance.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200, Internal Controls Standards

description: The date and time when this journal entry line record was first created in the system. Supports audit trails and data lineage.
The credit amount for this journal entry line in the functional currency. Null or zero if this is a debit line. Used for double-entry bookkeeping per GAAP.
The debit amount for this journal entry line in the functional currency. Null or zero if this is a credit line. Used for double-entry bookkeeping per GAAP.
Boolean indicator of whether this line item represents a direct cost (true) or an indirect/overhead cost (false). Used for cost allocation and grant compliance.
Classification of donor restrictions on the funds used in this line item per FASB ASC 958: unrestricted, temporarily restricted (time or purpose), or permanently restricted (endowment).
The effective date of the transaction represented by this line item. May differ from posting date for accruals, deferrals, and period adjustments.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the functional currency in which the debit and credit amounts are denominated.
reference: ISO 4217, FASB ASC 830 - Foreign Currency Matters

description: Classification of the expense by functional category as required by FASB ASC 958: program services, management and general, or fundraising. Used for Form 990 and donor reporting.
The Negotiated Indirect Cost Rate Agreement (NICRA) rate applied to this line item for Facilities and Administration (F&A) cost allocation per OMB Uniform Guidance.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200, NICRA Standards

description: Boolean indicator of whether this line item represents an intercompany or inter-entity transaction between organizational units or affiliated entities.
The vendor invoice number or internal invoice reference associated with this line item. Used for accounts payable reconciliation and audit trails.
reference: GAAP for Nonprofits, Accounts Payable Standards

description: Narrative description of the journal entry line item. Provides context and explanation for the transaction, supporting audit trails and financial transparency.
Sequential line number within the journal entry, used for ordering and referencing individual line items within the parent entry.
The user ID or name of the person who last modified this journal entry line. Supports audit trails and change tracking.
The date and time when this journal entry line record was last modified. Supports audit trails and change tracking.

The natural classification of the expense by type of expenditure (e.g., salaries, benefits, travel, supplies). Complements functional expense reporting per FASB ASC 958. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: salary|benefits|travel|supplies|equipment|occupancy|professional_services — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
The payment reference or check number associated with this line item for accounts payable or accounts receivable transactions. Supports cash reconciliation.
reference: GAAP for Nonprofits, Cash Management Standards

description: The date on which this journal entry line was posted to the general ledger. Used for period-end closing and financial reporting cutoff.
The profit center or revenue-generating unit associated with this line item. Used for revenue attribution and financial performance analysis by business unit.
The program or project code to which this line item is allocated. Supports program-level financial tracking and Results-Based Management (RBM) reporting.
Boolean indicator of whether this line item is a reversal or correction of a previous journal entry line. Used for audit trails and error correction tracking.
Reference to the source document or transaction that generated this journal entry line (e.g., invoice ID, payroll batch ID, grant disbursement ID). Supports audit trails.
Identifier of the source system or module from which this journal entry line originated (e.g., AP, AR, Payroll, Grant Management). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.
Tax code or VAT code applicable to this line item. Used for tax compliance, reporting, and recovery in jurisdictions where the nonprofit operates.
The user ID or name of the person who created this journal entry line. Supports audit trails and data lineage.

Reference to the grant or award against which this payable is charged. Critical for restricted fund tracking and grant financial management.
Reference to the organization's bank account from which payment was disbursed. Used for cash management and bank reconciliation.

Reference to the organizational cost center responsible for this expenditure. Used for budget control and cost allocation.

Foreign key linking to field.country_office. Business justification: Payables are processed at country office level. Finance needs to track payables by country office for country-level AP management and cash flow planning.

description: Foreign key linking to workforce.staff_member. Business justification: Finance tracks which staff member created each payable invoice for accountability and audit trail in accounts payable processing.

Foreign key linking to field.distribution_event. Business justification: Vendor invoices for distribution supplies/services link to specific distributions. Finance needs this for distribution cost tracking, audit trail, and distribution cost analysis.

Foreign key linking to field.field_deployment. Business justification: Deployment invoices (travel, accommodation, per diem) are payables tied to specific field deployments. Linking payable to field_deployment enables deployment cost tracking and budget vs. actuals for e

Reference to the general ledger account code in the Chart of Accounts (CoA) where this payable expense is recorded.
Reference to the goods receipt document confirming delivery of goods or services. Used in three-way match process. Null if goods receipt not applicable.

Foreign key linking to finance.grant_budget. Business justification: payable has is_grant_eligible (BOOLEAN) flag but no grant_budget_id FK. AP invoices charged to grants must reference the specific grant budget for expenditure tracking, prior approval threshold enforc

Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: payable has clearing_document_number (STRING) as a scalar reference but no journal_entry_id FK. Every AP invoice posting creates a journal entry in the GL (debit expense, credit AP liability). Adding

Foreign key linking to field.project_site. Business justification: Vendor invoices often relate to specific sites (site supplies, site services). Finance needs to track payables by site for site cost management and budget tracking.
Reference to the originating purchase order for three-way match validation (PO, goods receipt, invoice). Null for non-PO invoices.

Foreign key linking to compliance.sanctions_screening. Business justification: Vendor payments require sanctions screening before disbursement per OFAC/UN sanctions compliance. Treasury operations link each payable to screening result to document compliance and prevent prohibite

Foreign key linking to supply.shipment. Business justification: Freight forwarder and customs broker invoices (payables) reference specific shipments in NGO logistics. Linking payables to shipments enables freight cost reconciliation per shipment and supports duty

Foreign key linking to grant.subaward. Business justification: Payables are issued to subrecipients against specific subawards for disbursement processing. Finance tracks which subaward each payable belongs to for disbursement monitoring, FFATA reporting, subawar
Reference to the vendor or supplier from whom goods or services were procured. Links to the vendor master data.

Foreign key linking to supply.waybill. Business justification: Transport invoices (payables) are matched to waybills in NGO logistics finance — a 3-way match process (PO, waybill, invoice) for freight payments. Direct FK from payable to waybill enables transport
Current approval workflow status. Invoices must be approved before payment per internal controls and segregation of duties requirements.
User ID or name of the person who approved the invoice for payment. Required for audit trail and accountability per OMB Uniform Guidance.
Date the invoice was approved for payment. Used for workflow tracking and compliance reporting. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
Timestamp when the payable invoice record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Early payment discount or negotiated discount amount applied to the invoice. Reduces the net payable amount. In invoice currency.
Date the invoice was flagged as disputed. Used for vendor relationship management and dispute resolution tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
Reason code or description if the invoice is disputed (e.g., pricing discrepancy, quantity mismatch, quality issue). Null if not disputed.
Date by which payment is due per the payment terms. Used for cash flow planning and to avoid late payment penalties. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
Exchange rate used to convert invoice currency to the organization's functional currency (typically USD for US-based nonprofits). Rate at invoice date or payment date per accounting policy.
Invoice net amount converted to the organization's functional currency using the exchange rate. Used for general ledger posting and financial reporting.
Negotiated Indirect Cost Rate Agreement (NICRA) rate applied to this expenditure for Facilities and Administration (F&A) cost allocation. Critical for federal grant compliance.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the invoice is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency operations and exchange rate tracking.
Date the vendor issued the invoice. Used to calculate payment terms and aging. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
Free-text description of the goods or services covered by this invoice. Provides business context for the expenditure.
Total invoice amount before taxes and adjustments. Base amount for goods or services procured, in invoice currency.
Net payable amount after applying taxes, withholding tax, and discounts. This is the amount to be paid to the vendor. In invoice currency.
Vendor-provided invoice number. Business identifier for the payable transaction. Must be unique per vendor for audit trail and reconciliation.
Date the invoice was received by the organization. Used for processing time tracking and internal controls. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
Flag indicating whether this expenditure is allowable and allocable to the associated grant per OMB Uniform Guidance cost principles. True if eligible, False if not.
Flag indicating whether this payable is charged to a restricted fund with donor-imposed limitations. True if restricted, False if unrestricted. Critical for FASB ASC 958 compliance.
Timestamp when the payable invoice record was last modified. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
Actual date the payment was disbursed to the vendor. Null if payment has not yet been made. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
System-generated payment document number or check number. Links the payable to the actual payment transaction for reconciliation and audit.
Method used to disburse payment to the vendor. Critical for cash management, bank reconciliation, and audit trail documentation.

Current lifecycle status of the payable invoice in the procure-to-pay process. Tracks progression from invoice receipt through payment disbursement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|scheduled|partially_paid|paid|disputed|cancelled|on_hold — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Contractual payment terms agreed with the vendor (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30). Determines due date calculation and early payment discount eligibility.
Total tax amount charged on the invoice (VAT, sales tax, GST, etc.). In invoice currency. Used for tax reporting and compliance.
Status of the three-way match validation comparing purchase order, goods receipt, and invoice. Matched status is required for automated payment approval per internal controls.
Amount of tax withheld at source per local tax regulations. Reduces the net payment to vendor. In invoice currency.
Unique identifier for the accounts payable payment transaction. Primary key for the payable payment record.
Identifier of the grant or award that is funding this payment. Links to the grant master data for grant expenditure tracking and donor reporting compliance.

description: ISO 4217 three-letter code for the organization's functional reporting currency (e.g., USD). Used alongside exchange_rate to express payroll costs in a consistent currency for consolidated financial statements and donor reporting.

reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; USAID BHA Reporting; Unit4 ERP Grant Management; IATI

description: The Indirect Cost Rate (ICR) or F&A (Facilities and Administration) rate applied to direct labor costs in this payroll run, as defined in the organization's NICRA (Negotiated Indirect Cost Rate Agreement). Used to calculate indirect cost recovery charges against grants.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; NICRA; USAID BHA; FASB ASC 958

description: Indicates whether this payroll run includes retroactive pay adjustments for prior pay periods (e.g., backdated salary increases, correction of underpayments). Retroactive runs require special grant cost allocation treatment under OMB Uniform Guidance.
reference: Workday HCM; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; FASB ASC 958

description: The date and time when this payroll run record was most recently modified. Tracks corrections, status changes, and amendments to the payroll run. Essential for data lineage and change management in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: Workday HCM; FASB ASC 958; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: Free-text field for payroll officers to document special circumstances, exceptions, or explanatory remarks about this payroll run (e.g., reason for off-cycle run, description of corrections made, emergency payment context). Supports audit trail and knowledge transfer.
reference: Workday HCM; CHS (Core Humanitarian Standard); OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: The cadence at which employees in this payroll group are paid. Determines the number of pay periods per year and affects annualized salary calculations, benefit proration, and grant budget reporting.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; FASB ASC 958; Local Labor Law Standards

description: The last calendar date of the pay period covered by this payroll run. Defines the closing boundary of the earnings window. Together with pay_period_start_date, determines the scope of labor costs chargeable to grants and programs.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; FASB ASC 958

description: The first calendar date of the pay period covered by this payroll run. Defines the beginning of the earnings window for all employees included in the run. Used for time-and-attendance reconciliation and grant cost allocation.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; FASB ASC 958

description: The scheduled or actual date on which net pay is disbursed to employees' bank accounts or via alternative payment methods. Used for cash flow planning, bank reconciliation, and compliance with local labor law payment deadlines.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; IRS 501(c)(3) Regulations; Local Labor Law Standards

description: The disbursement method used to pay employees in this payroll run. In humanitarian contexts, mobile money (e.g., M-Pesa) is common for field staff in remote areas. Determines the payment processing channel and reconciliation approach.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; Local Banking Regulations; OCHA Cash and Voucher Assistance Standards

description: Classification of the staff population covered by this payroll run. Distinguishes between national staff (locally hired), international staff (globally mobile), expatriates (expat), volunteers with stipends, and consultants. Drives applicable tax regimes, benefit structures, and compliance reporting requirements.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; IRS 501(c)(3) Regulations; NICRA (Negotiated Indirect Cost Rate Agreement)

description: The name or employee identifier of the payroll officer who initiated and processed this payroll run in Workday HCM. Supports RACI (Responsible Accountable Consulted Informed) accountability tracking and audit trail requirements.
reference: Workday HCM; RACI Framework; CHS; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: The start date of the prior period for which retroactive pay adjustments are being made in this payroll run. Populated only when is_retroactive is True. Used to correctly allocate retroactive costs to the appropriate grant and fiscal period.
reference: Workday HCM; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; FASB ASC 958

description: Externally-known business identifier for the payroll run, typically formatted as PR-YYYY-MM-NNNN (e.g., PR-2024-03-0012). Used in audit trails, finance reconciliation, and donor/grant reporting to reference a specific payroll cycle.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; FASB ASC 958

description: Current lifecycle state of the payroll run within Workday HCM. Progresses from draft through calculation, approval, and payment. Cancelled status indicates the run was voided before disbursement. Used for workflow control and audit compliance.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; CHS (Core Humanitarian Standard); FASB ASC 958

description: Categorizes the nature of the payroll run. Regular runs follow the standard pay schedule; off-cycle runs address missed payments; supplemental runs cover bonuses or allowances; correction runs fix prior errors; final runs process termination pay. Determines processing rules and audit requirements.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; FASB ASC 958; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: The aggregate of all deductions applied across employees in this payroll run, including statutory taxes, social security contributions, health insurance premiums, pension contributions, and voluntary deductions. Reconciled against payroll liabilities in the General Ledger (GL).
reference: FASB ASC 958; IRS 501(c)(3) Regulations; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; Workday HCM

description: The aggregate employer-side payroll costs for this run, including employer social security, pension matching, health insurance, and other statutory employer contributions. Represents the full labor cost burden beyond gross pay, critical for grant budget management and F&A (Facilities and Administration) cost allocation.
reference: FASB ASC 958; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; NICRA; Workday HCM

description: The aggregate gross earnings for all employees included in this payroll run before any deductions. Includes base salary, allowances, overtime, and other earnings components. Used for BvA (Budget versus Actual) reporting, grant cost allocation, and financial stewardship.
reference: FASB ASC 958; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; Workday HCM Payroll Module

description: The aggregate net amount disbursed to all employees in this payroll run after all deductions. Equals total_gross_pay minus total_deductions. Used for cash disbursement planning, bank transfer reconciliation, and treasury management.
reference: FASB ASC 958; Workday HCM Payroll Module; SAP S/4HANA AP

description: The aggregate income tax and statutory levies withheld from employee earnings in this payroll run. Reported to tax authorities and reconciled against payroll tax liability accounts in the CoA (Chart of Accounts). Supports compliance with IRS and local tax authority reporting.
reference: IRS 501(c)(3) Regulations; Local Tax Authority Standards; FASB ASC 958; Workday HCM

description: The native identifier assigned to this payroll run by Workday HCM, the system of record. Enables traceability back to the source system for audit, reconciliation, and support purposes. Distinct from the Silver layer surrogate key payroll_run_id.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each individual payslip record in the Workday HCM payroll system. Serves as the primary key for the payslip data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.

Foreign key linking to grant.award. Business justification: Effort certification and grant expenditure reporting (2 CFR 200, USAID ADS) require linking payslips to the funding award. 'grant_code' is a denormalized plain-text award reference. This FK enables ac

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Individual payslips are charged to cost centers for functional expense allocation and grant compliance; FK enables direct cost reporting per cost center and supports effort certification requirements.

Foreign key linking to workforce.employment_contract. Business justification: A payslip is generated based on the terms of an employment contract (base salary, allowances, deductions, salary grade). Linking payslip to employment_contract enables audit trails showing which contr

Foreign key linking to finance.finance_fund. Business justification: Payslips funded by specific grants require fund-level tracking for donor reporting and effort certification compliance; FK enables fund-level personnel cost reporting required by USAID, EU, and other

Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Payslip salary components (gross pay, benefits, taxes) post to specific GL accounts; FK enables automated journal generation, financial statement accuracy, and direct audit trail from individual paysl

Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Individual payslips are posted via journal entries; FK replaces the denormalized gl_journal_reference text field and provides direct audit trail from payslip to GL posting, essential for NGO financial

Reference to the original payslip record that this corrected or reversed payslip supersedes. Null for standard payslips; populated only when is_correction is True, enabling full audit trail of payroll corrections.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; FASB ASC 958; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: Reference to the payroll run batch under which this payslip was processed. Enables traceability of individual payslips back to the parent payroll run for audit and reconciliation purposes.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: Reference to the staff member for whom this payslip was generated. Links to the employee master record in Workday HCM HRIS.

Date and time when the payroll run containing this payslip was formally approved in the Workday HCM approval workflow. Provides an auditable approval event timestamp for statutory and donor compliance.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: Name or identifier of the authorising manager or finance officer who approved this payroll run in the Workday HCM approval chain. Supports RACI accountability documentation and donor audit requirements.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; CHS Alliance

description: Masked or tokenised reference to the employee's bank account details used for payment disbursement. Full account details are stored securely in Workday HCM; only a reference token is retained in the Silver Layer for audit traceability without exposing PCI-sensitive data.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; PCI DSS; GDPR Article 25 (Data Minimisation)

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the duty station where the employee is based and where payroll is processed. Determines applicable labour law, tax jurisdiction, statutory deduction rules, and local currency for multi-country NGO operations across 50+ countries.
reference: ISO 3166-1; IATI Standard; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: Date and time when this payslip record was first created in the Workday HCM system and ingested into the Databricks Silver Layer. Provides the audit creation timestamp for data lineage and compliance tracking.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; IATI Standard; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: Employer's contribution to the employee's pension or provident fund for the pay period. Recorded separately from employee deductions for total employment cost reporting and grant cost allocation under OMB Uniform Guidance.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; Local Pension Regulations

description: Employer's statutory social security or national insurance contribution associated with this payslip. Not deducted from employee pay but recorded for total employment cost reporting, BvA grant cost allocation, and NICRA indirect cost rate calculations.
reference: Local Labour Law; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; FASB ASC 958

description: Foreign exchange rate applied to convert local currency salary amounts to the payment currency at the time of payroll processing. Critical for multi-currency reconciliation and donor financial reporting.
reference: FASB ASC 958; IATI Standard; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: Allowance specific to internationally-recruited expatriate staff, covering relocation, cost-of-living differentials, and international assignment premiums. Applicable only to staff in the expat payroll group.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; INGO Expat Compensation Standards

description: Allowance paid to staff working in active field operations, remote areas, or humanitarian response zones. Distinct from hardship allowance; compensates for operational field conditions and mobility requirements.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; SPHERE Standards; CHS Alliance

description: Total gross salary amount in local currency before any deductions, representing the employee's base contractual pay for the pay period. Forms the basis for all statutory deduction calculations and BvA payroll cost reporting.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; FASB ASC 958; GAAP for Nonprofits

description: Additional allowance paid to staff deployed in hardship or non-family duty stations as classified by UN/INGO hardship classification frameworks. Compensates for difficult living conditions in humanitarian field operations.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; UN Common System Hardship Classification; INGO HR Standards

description: Monetary allowance paid to the employee to cover accommodation costs, commonly provided to international and expat staff in high-cost or insecure duty stations. Taxability varies by country and employment contract type.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; INGO Compensation Frameworks

description: Statutory income tax withheld from the employee's gross pay in accordance with the applicable national tax legislation of the duty station country. Reported to tax authorities and included in statutory payroll filings.
reference: IRS 501(c)(3) Regulations; Local Tax Authority Requirements; GAAP for Nonprofits

description: Indicates whether this payslip is a corrected reissue of a previously reversed payslip. When True, the original_payslip_id field references the superseded payslip for audit trail continuity.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; FASB ASC 958

description: Indicates whether this payslip was generated as part of an off-cycle payroll run (e.g., for a new hire mid-period, a termination payment, or a correction) rather than the standard scheduled payroll cycle.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the local currency in which the employee's salary components are denominated and calculated. Supports multi-currency payroll operations across 50+ countries.

Final take-home pay amount in local currency after all statutory and voluntary deductions have been subtracted from gross pay and allowances. Represents the amount the employee is entitled to receive for the pay period.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; FASB ASC 958; GAAP for Nonprofits

description: Net pay amount converted to the payment currency using the applicable exchange rate. Represents the actual amount disbursed to the employee's bank account. May equal net_pay_local when local and payment currencies are the same.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; ISO 4217; FASB ASC 958

description: The last calendar date of the payroll period covered by this payslip. Together with pay_period_start_date defines the exact payroll cycle window for statutory and donor reporting.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; FASB ASC 958; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: The first calendar date of the payroll period covered by this payslip. Used for Budget vs Actual (BvA) payroll cost allocation to grants and programs.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; FASB ASC 958; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the currency in which net pay is actually disbursed to the employee. May differ from local_currency_code for expatriate staff paid in USD or EUR regardless of duty station.
reference: ISO 4217; Workday HCM Payroll Module; INGO Expat Compensation Standards

description: The actual date on which net pay was disbursed to the employee's bank account or via the designated payment method. Represents the principal business event date for this transaction.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; GAAP for Nonprofits

description: Method by which net pay is disbursed to the employee. Bank transfer is standard for most staff; cash and mobile money are used in remote field locations with limited banking infrastructure, common in humanitarian operations.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; CHS Alliance Field Operations Standards

description: Classification of the staff member's employment category for payroll processing purposes. Determines applicable pay scales, allowances, tax treatment, and statutory deduction rules. National staff follow local labour law; international and expat staff follow INGO compensation frameworks.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; INGO HR Standards

description: Sequential run number within the payroll period (e.g., 1 for the primary run, 2 for a supplemental or correction run). Distinguishes regular payroll runs from off-cycle or adjustment runs.

Externally-visible, human-readable unique reference number for this payslip, typically formatted as PSL-{COUNTRY}-{YEAR}-{MONTH}-{SEQUENCE}. Used in employee communications, statutory filings, and donor audit documentation.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; FASB ASC 958

description: Current lifecycle state of this individual payslip record. Distinguishes between payslips that have been generated and issued to the employee versus those that have been reversed or superseded by a corrected payslip.

Employee contribution to pension or provident fund deducted from gross pay. Includes both mandatory statutory pension contributions and voluntary supplementary pension scheme contributions where applicable.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; Local Pension Regulations; GAAP for Nonprofits

description: Identifier of the humanitarian or development program to which this payroll cost is allocated. Supports program-level cost tracking for MEL reporting, LogFrame financial monitoring, and donor accountability.
reference: Unit4 ERP Project Accounting; IATI Standard; FASB ASC 958

description: Current lifecycle state of the payroll run associated with this payslip. Tracks the workflow from initial calculation through approval, payment disbursement, and any reversals required for corrections.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; FASB ASC 958

description: Statutory social security or national insurance contribution deducted from the employee's pay as required by local labour law. Varies by country and employment type; may be zero for certain expat staff under bilateral social security agreements.
reference: Local Labour Law; GAAP for Nonprofits; Workday HCM Payroll Module

description: Sum of all allowance components (housing, hardship, field, expat, transport, and any other applicable allowances) for the pay period in local currency. Used as a subtotal in payslip presentation and BvA reporting.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; FASB ASC 958

description: Sum of all mandatory statutory deductions (income tax, social security, pension) for the pay period. Used as a subtotal in payslip presentation and for statutory remittance reconciliation.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; FASB ASC 958; Local Tax Authority Requirements

description: Allowance paid to cover employee commuting or field transportation costs. Commonly provided to national staff in urban and peri-urban duty stations as part of the total compensation package.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; Local Labour Law Standards

description: Date and time when this payslip record was last modified in the source system or Silver Layer. Tracks corrections, status changes, and reprocessing events for data lineage and audit purposes.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; FASB ASC 958

description: Total of all employee-elected voluntary deductions for the pay period, including staff association fees, health insurance premiums, loan repayments, savings scheme contributions, and other authorised deductions.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; FASB ASC 958

description: Unique system-generated identifier for the performance review record. Primary key for the performance_review data product in the Workday HCM-sourced workforce domain.

Reference to the primary grant funding the employee's position during the review period. Supports donor-funded staff performance reporting requirements under USAID, DFID, CERF, and OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.
reference: Unit4 ERP Grant Financial Management; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; USAID; DFID

description: Reference to the primary humanitarian or development program to which the employee was assigned during the review period. Links performance outcomes to program results for RBM and donor reporting.
reference: Microsoft Dynamics 365 for Nonprofits Program Management; RBM Results-Based Management; IATI

description: Reference to the organizational position held by the employee at the time of the review, capturing role context for benchmarking and talent analytics.
Reference to the staff member (reviewee) who is the subject of this performance review. Links to the employee master record in Workday HCM.
reference: Workday HCM Worker Object; FASB ASC 958

description: Reference to the staff member (manager or designated reviewer) who conducted and submitted this performance review. Supports Results-Based Management (RBM) accountability chains.
reference: Workday HCM Performance Management Module; CHS Core Humanitarian Standard

description: Rating assessing the employee's demonstrated accountability to affected populations and beneficiaries, a core CHS and HAP requirement for all humanitarian staff. Reflects adherence to feedback mechanisms and complaint handling.
reference: CHS Core Humanitarian Standard Commitment 4; HAP Humanitarian Accountability Partnership; Sphere Association

description: Rating for the collaboration and partnership competency, assessing the employee's effectiveness in working across teams, with partner organizations (CSOs, CBOs, INGOs), and with beneficiary communities.
reference: Workday HCM Competency Framework; CHS Core Humanitarian Standard

description: Composite numeric score reflecting the employee's average rating across all behavioral competencies assessed in the review (e.g., leadership, collaboration, accountability, humanitarian principles). Supports talent development analytics.
reference: Workday HCM Competency Framework; CHS Core Humanitarian Standard Commitment 8

description: The date and time when the performance review record was first created in the system (Workday HCM or the Databricks Silver Layer ingestion). Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.
reference: Workday HCM Audit Trail; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; FASB ASC 958

description: Structured recommendations from the reviewer for the employee's professional development, including suggested training, mentoring, stretch assignments, or Learning Management System (LMS) courses to address identified gaps.
reference: Workday HCM Learning Module; LMS Learning Management System; CHS Core Humanitarian Standard Commitment 8

description: The geographic location (country, city, or field base) where the employee was deployed during the review period. Critical for field operations context, hardship allowance eligibility, and OCHA/USAID/BHA donor reporting.
reference: OCHA Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs; USAID Bureau for Humanitarian Assistance (BHA); Workday HCM Location

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the country where the employee was stationed during the review period. Enables geographic workforce analytics and cross-country performance benchmarking.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; IATI International Aid Transparency Initiative; OCHA

description: Indicates whether the employee has formally acknowledged the performance review in Workday HCM. Required for HR audit compliance and CHS accountability to affected populations standards.
reference: Workday HCM Performance Review Acknowledgment; CHS Core Humanitarian Standard Commitment 4; HAP

description: The date on which the reviewed employee formally acknowledged receipt and review of their performance appraisal in Workday HCM. Required for HAP and CHS accountability compliance.
reference: Workday HCM Performance Management Module; HAP Humanitarian Accountability Partnership; CHS Core Humanitarian Standard Commitment 4

description: Free-text comments submitted by the employee at the time of acknowledgment, capturing their response to the review outcome, including any disagreements, additional context, or commitments for the next period.
reference: Workday HCM Performance Review; CHS Core Humanitarian Standard Commitment 4

description: Indicates whether the employee formally disputed or disagreed with the performance review outcome. Triggers the HR grievance and appeals process in accordance with organizational HR policy and CHS accountability standards.
reference: CHS Core Humanitarian Standard Commitment 4; HAP Humanitarian Accountability Partnership; IRS 501(c)(3) HR Compliance

description: Free-text self-assessment narrative submitted by the employee prior to the review meeting, documenting their own perspective on achievements, challenges, and development needs during the review period.
reference: Workday HCM Performance Review Self-Evaluation; CHS Core Humanitarian Standard Commitment 4

description: The date and time when the performance review record was most recently modified in the system. Tracks the full edit history for audit compliance and data quality monitoring in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: Workday HCM Audit Trail; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; FASB ASC 958

description: Rating for the leadership competency dimension, assessing the employee's ability to guide teams, make decisions under pressure, and drive program outcomes in humanitarian field contexts.
reference: Workday HCM Competency Framework; CHS Core Humanitarian Standard Commitment 8

description: The planned date for the employee's next performance review or check-in, as agreed during the current review meeting. Supports proactive HR scheduling and talent management planning.

Composite percentage score (0–100) reflecting the degree to which the employee achieved their defined performance objectives during the review period. Derived from individual objective ratings in Workday HCM and used in RBM and LogFrame reporting.
reference: Workday HCM Goals Management; RBM Results-Based Management; LogFrame Logical Framework

description: The holistic performance rating assigned to the employee for the review period, reflecting the reviewer's consolidated assessment across all competencies and objectives. Drives talent retention, promotion, and RBM reporting decisions.
reference: Workday HCM Rating Scale Configuration; FASB ASC 958; CHS Core Humanitarian Standard

description: Numeric score corresponding to the overall performance rating on the configured rating scale (e.g., 1.00–5.00). Enables quantitative talent analytics, benchmarking, and Results-Based Management (RBM) reporting.
reference: Workday HCM Performance Rating Scale; RBM Results-Based Management Framework

description: Indicates whether a formal Performance Improvement Plan (PIP) has been recommended or initiated as a result of this review, typically triggered by an unsatisfactory or needs_improvement overall rating.
reference: Workday HCM Performance Management; IRS 501(c)(3) HR Compliance; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: Indicates whether the reviewer recommends the employee for promotion or advancement to a higher grade or responsibility level based on this review cycle's performance outcomes.
reference: Workday HCM Talent Management; FASB ASC 958

description: Version identifier of the performance rating scale applied in this review (e.g., 'v2.1-2024'). Ensures historical comparability as rating scales evolve over time.
reference: Workday HCM Performance Rating Scale Configuration

description: Indicates whether the reviewer or HR has flagged this employee as at risk of leaving the organization, enabling proactive talent retention interventions. Supports NGO workforce stability in high-turnover humanitarian contexts.
reference: Workday HCM Talent Management; CHS Core Humanitarian Standard Commitment 8

description: Classification of the performance review cycle that this appraisal belongs to. Distinguishes annual appraisals, mid-year check-ins, probationary reviews, end-of-contract assessments, and ad hoc reviews triggered by specific events.
reference: Workday HCM Performance Review Cycle Configuration; CHS Core Humanitarian Standard Commitment 8

description: The deadline by which the performance review must be completed and submitted by the reviewer. Used for HR compliance tracking and manager accountability reporting.
reference: Workday HCM Performance Management Module; CHS Core Humanitarian Standard

description: The date on which the formal performance review discussion meeting took place between the reviewer and the employee. Distinct from the submission date; required for CHS accountability documentation.
reference: Workday HCM Performance Management; CHS Core Humanitarian Standard Commitment 4

description: Externally-visible, human-readable business identifier for the performance review record (e.g., PR-2024-000123). Used in HR correspondence, audit trails, and donor-funded program compliance reporting.
reference: Workday HCM Performance Management Module; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: The last day of the performance period being evaluated in this review. Together with review_period_start_date, defines the full appraisal window.
reference: Workday HCM Performance Period Configuration; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: The first day of the performance period being evaluated in this review. Defines the temporal boundary for objective achievement and behavioral observation.
reference: Workday HCM Performance Period Configuration; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: Current lifecycle state of the performance review workflow in Workday HCM. Tracks progression from draft through manager completion to employee acknowledgment and final closure.
reference: Workday HCM Business Process Framework; FASB ASC 958

description: The date on which the reviewer formally submitted the completed performance review in Workday HCM. Used to measure timeliness compliance against the review due date.

Free-text qualitative narrative provided by the reviewer summarizing the employee's performance, key achievements, areas for improvement, and behavioral observations during the review period. Confidential HR record.
reference: Workday HCM Performance Review; FASB ASC 958; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: Classification of the employee's staff category at the time of the review. Distinguishes national staff, international staff, expatriates, consultants, and volunteers, which affects applicable HR policies, review templates, and donor reporting requirements.
reference: Workday HCM Worker Type Configuration; OCHA Humanitarian Workforce Standards; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: Rating for the employee's technical competency relevant to their functional role (e.g., MEAL methodology, WASH engineering, GBV case management, financial stewardship, ICT4D). Supports targeted learning and development planning.
reference: Workday HCM Competency Framework; SPHERE Humanitarian Standards; IATI International Aid Transparency Initiative

description: Rating assessing the degree to which the employee demonstrates alignment with the NGO's core organizational values and humanitarian principles (e.g., humanity, neutrality, impartiality, accountability). Critical for CHS compliance and organizational culture.
reference: CHS Core Humanitarian Standard; Sphere Association Humanitarian Standards; HAP Humanitarian Accountability Partnership

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each staff assignment record in Workday HCM. Serves as the primary key for the staff_assignment data product.

Reference to the geographic duty station or field office where the staff member is deployed for this assignment. Supports field operations tracking and hardship/hazard pay calculations.

Foreign key linking to finance.finance_fund. Business justification: Staff assignments are funded by specific grants/funds; FK enables fund-level effort tracking and donor-required effort certification reporting, critical for USAID and other institutional donor complia
Reference to the humanitarian or development program to which the staff member is assigned. Enables program-level workforce planning and MEL accountability mapping.
reference: Microsoft Dynamics 365 for Nonprofits — Program Management

description: Foreign key linking to workforce.job_profile. Business justification: A staff assignment captures the role being performed during the assignment period. job_profile is the SSOT for role definitions including competencies, pay grade, and responsibilities. Adding job_prof

Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Staff assignments to programs, projects, or grants should reference the organizational unit for proper cost allocation, reporting hierarchy, and matrix management visibility. While staff_assignment ha
